 ⏺ Rejecting v1 and rebuilding. Stopping to think through the whole plan as a system before touching anything.

 ---

 What I got wrong in v1 (the foundation)

 1. Wrong schema shape for the claude.ai round-trip. I proposed campaigns/<slug>/config.yaml + variants/<role>.md + a YAML loader. That schema forces Steven to translate claude.ai's natural output (one

 conversational markdown blob) into a multi-file directory structure on every round trip. Friction at the worst point in the workflow. The correct shape is a single markdown file per campaign with frontmatter +

 ## variant: <role> sections, matching what claude.ai naturally produces. Copy-paste-save-run.

 2. Wrong choice for the role classifier. I proposed a rule-based classifier using target_roles.py tier lists as seed data. Rule-based classifiers on job titles cap out at maybe 75-85% accuracy (estimate —

 ambiguous-title cases like "Director", "Coordinator", "Specialist" break deterministic rules) and require 150-300 lines of regex-plus-fallbacks to get there. Haiku at temp=0 will hit 92-97% (estimate) in 50

 lines of code for a per-contact cost under a penny (estimate — based on Haiku pricing and typical title length). Rule-based isn't "simpler" — it's a maintenance burden disguised as simplicity.

 3. Including the diocesan refactor was a production-risk blunder. The Wed 2026-04-15 drip runs tomorrow and the Thu 2026-04-16 drip runs the day after (measured — from CLAUDE.md CURRENT STATE). Refactoring

 live production machinery mid-week, when the refactor is cosmetic and not required for any new campaign, is exactly the sort of thing that turns a clean plan into a Monday-morning incident. Kill it from the

 plan entirely. Diocesan drip stays untouched. The new loader lives alongside it. When diocesan next needs a change, that's when the migration happens.

 4. Too many commits, overly ceremonial. Six commits with a schema-only commit, a separate docs commit, and a refactor commit that was already being cut. Collapse to four : parser + schema, role classifier,

 export utility, CLI orchestrator. Docs update rides on the last commit. No standalone "schema" commit — the parser test IS the schema validation.

 5. Misnamed the script. sequence_drip.py implies time-spaced sends. Most campaigns are one-day bulk loads. The generalization is a campaign loader , not a drip. Rename to scripts/load_campaign.py .

 6. Punted the hardest UX question. How does Steven get research-engine contacts INTO a campaign file? I handwaved "CSV". Reality: Steven is not going to CSV-export from Sheets for every campaign. The loader

 needs to accept stdin (paste) , path to a CSV , OR a Google Sheets tab reference . stdin is load-bearing — it's what makes the claude.ai round-trip work without an intermediate file.

 7. Sidecar state mishandled. I wrote "store sequence_ids back into config.yaml" — which means mutating a source-of-truth file after --create runs, which is fragile and breaks git diffs. Correct pattern:

 sidecar state file at campaigns/<slug>.state.json (gitignored), separate from the campaign markdown.

 8. Missing: pre-flight validator that surfaces ALL failures at once. outreach_client.validate_sequence_inputs returns failures one variant at a time. If Steven's campaign file has 5 variants and 4 have

 validator failures, he discovers them one-at-a-time across four --create runs. The loader needs to dry-run every variant through validate_sequence_inputs before any POST hits Outreach , and print all failures

 in one output. This is a required UX primitive, not a nice-to-have.

 9. Missing: idempotency on --create . Running it twice shouldn't produce duplicate sequences. The sidecar state file solves this — skip variants whose sequence_id is already set.

 10. Missing: what happens when Steven edits copy mid-campaign. After --create runs, the sequence is live in Outreach. Editing campaigns/cue_2026.md doesn't update Outreach. v1 needs to warn on this (compare

 state-file snapshot hash to current file hash) and leave --update as a v2 feature.

 ⏺ Updated plan

 ⎿  /plan to preview

 ⏺ User approved Claude's plan

 ⎿  Plan saved to: ~/.claude/plans/luminous-honking-cook.md · /plan to edit

 Plan — Generalized Campaign Loader with Role Segmentation and Claude.ai Round-Trip

 Rev 2 — rebuilt from scratch after v1 pressure-test (2026-04-14 S64 plan mode)

 ---

 Context

 Steven's production workflow for campaign loads is:

 1. Draft copy and subjects (starter comes from either the handler auto-draft at agent/main.py:482 or an existing Outreach sequence copied out as a starter).

 2. Iterate the copy in claude.ai — that's the preferred drafting surface because the editing UX beats Claude Code for prose work.

 3. Drop contacts into Claude Code.

 4. Classify each contact by role/avatar (admin / curriculum / IT / teacher / coach / other).

 5. Produce role-variant copy sets (one per bucket).

 6. Call outreach_client.create_sequence per variant to create real live sequences in Outreach.

 7. Steven verifies and activates each sequence manually in the Outreach UI (Rule 15 — all sequences are drafts until Steven signs off).

 8. Claude Code loads the right contacts into the right sequences, with IANA timezone enforcement and same-district stagger (via execute_load_plan sleep_seconds, already built).

 This pattern has been hand-coded three times in Scout history: S38 CUE conference loader ( scripts/enrich_cue_leads.py , 2,422 lines, commit 7d9a539 ), S43 C4 cold license request loader

 ( scripts/create_c4_sequences.py + enrich_c4_pass2.py , 1,622 lines combined, commit 353b1f0 ), and S44 webinar registrant tagging ( aabdb0c ). The S61 library promotion ( fdf6920 ) built create_prospect /

 find_prospect_by_email / add_prospect_to_sequence / build_load_plan / execute_load_plan but stopped short of the orchestration layer — the diocesan drip is the only committed campaign runner, and it's

 diocesan-specific at the CLI boundary (6 hardcoded sequence IDs 2008-2013, hardcoded DRIP_DAYS, hardcoded MAILBOX_ID).

 Intended outcome: ship a strategy-agnostic campaign loader so the next CUE / C4 / webinar / bond-trigger push uses library code instead of spawning a 4th ephemeral script. Rule 18 is the driving constraint

 — "promote ephemeral patterns before using them."

 Rule compliance:

 - Rule 1 — plan-mode required (this file).

 - Rule 7 — kill switches per component.

 - Rule 8 — one commit per feature.

 - Rule 11 — loader is a sync CLI script, not async; no time.sleep /async conflict.

 - Rule 15 — loader creates sequences but never activates; Steven activates manually in Outreach UI.

 - Rule 17 — timezone enforcement already handled at validate_prospect_inputs boundary.

 - Rule 18 — this plan IS the promotion; the promoted pattern is a single entry point ( scripts/load_campaign.py ) that future work reuses.

 - Rule 19 — loader output translates all Outreach IDs to human names before printing; no numeric IDs in stdout.

 ---

 Architectural decisions (locked)

 Decision 1 — Single markdown file per campaign, not a directory

 A campaign lives at campaigns/<slug>.md — one file, YAML frontmatter for metadata, ## variant: <role> H2 sections for each role's copy.

 Why: claude.ai produces conversational markdown. Directory-per-campaign with config.yaml + variants/*.md forces Steven to translate claude.ai output into a new schema on every round trip. Single-file lets

 him copy-paste-save.

 Schema (concrete, committed):

 ---

 campaign _name: "CUE 2026"

 campaign_ slug: "cue _2026"

 schedule_ id: 50 # C4 Tue-Thu

 drip _days:

 - 2026-04-21

 - 2026-04-22

 - 2026-04-23

 tag_ template: "cue-2026-{role}"

 sleep _seconds_ min: 60 # per-POST stagger, seconds

 sleep _seconds_ max: 180

 ---

 ## variant: admin

 target _role_ label: "Superintendent / Principal"

 num _steps: 5

 ### Step 1 — Subject: Quick note on your CS rollout at {{company}}

 Hi {{first_ name}},

 ...

 ### Step 2 — Subject: Re: Quick note on your CS rollout at {{company}}

 ...

 ## variant: teacher

 target _role_ label: "CS Teacher / Math Teacher"

 num _steps: 5

 ### Step 1 — Subject: ...

 ...

 What's NOT in the frontmatter: mailbox_id and owner_id . Both are hardcoded to 11 in outreach_client (Steven is the only user). Putting them in the schema adds surface area Steven will forget to set.

 Parser contract ( tools/campaign_file.py ):

 @dataclass

 class CampaignStep:

 subject: str

 body_html: str # parsed from the markdown body — plain text acceptable

 interval_seconds: int # default from a constant, not in schema

 @dataclass

 class CampaignVariant:

 role: str # "admin" | "curriculum" | "it" | "teacher" | "coach" | "other"

 target_role_label: str

 num_steps: int

 steps: list [CampaignStep]

 @dataclass

 class Campaign:

 name: str

 slug: str

 schedule_id: int

 drip_days: list [date]

 tag_template: str

 sleep_seconds: tuple [ int , int ]

 variants: dict [ str , CampaignVariant]

 def load_campaign(path: Path) -> Campaign:

 """Parse campaigns/<slug>.md into a Campaign. Permissive markdown parser."""

 def dump_campaign(campaign: Campaign) -> str :

 """Inverse — for export_sequence_for_editing round-trip."""

 Decision 2 — Claude Haiku for role classification, not rule-based

 tools/role_classifier.py calls Haiku at temp=0 with a six-bucket prompt per contact, results cached by SHA1 of the normalized title to avoid re-calling on reruns.

 Why: rule-based on job titles caps at 75-85% (estimate — ambiguous-title failure modes like "Director" / "Coordinator" / "Specialist"). Haiku at temp=0 should hit 92-97% (estimate). Cost estimate: under

 $0.002 per contact (estimate — Haiku input ~400 tokens, output ~20 tokens, at Haiku 4.5 pricing). A 1,119-contact C4 batch costs under $3 (estimate) — well inside the $25/week research budget.

 Interface:

 def classify_contact_role(contact: dict , *, cache_path: Path | None = None ) -> str :

 """

 Return one of: admin, curriculum, it, teacher, coach, other.

 Never raises — unclassifiable contacts return 'other'.

 """

 Pre-filter: call agent.target_roles.is_relevant_role(title) first. If False → return other without calling Claude. Saves cost on trades / IT_INFRA rows.

 Bucket definitions (in the Haiku prompt, not in code):

 ┌────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

 ┐

 │ Bucket │ Includes

 │

 ├────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

 ┤

 │ admin │ Superintendents, Principals, Assistant Principals, Asst Supts, Executive Directors, Heads of School, District Office senior leadership

 │

 ├────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

 ┤

 │ curriculum │ Curriculum Directors, C&I, Academic Coaches, Instructional Coaches, Assessment Coordinators, CS Curriculum Leads

 │

 ├────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

 ┤

 │ it │ Directors of Technology, CIOs, CTOs, EdTech Coordinators, Instructional Technology Coordinators (explicitly NOT help desk / sysadmin — those fall through to other via is_relevant_role

 │

 │ │ pre-filter)

 │

 ├────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

 ┤

 │ teacher │ CS Teachers, Math Teachers, Algebra Teachers, STEM Teachers, Robotics Teachers, classroom-level roles

 │

 ├────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

 ┤

 │ coach │ Athletic Coaches, Esports Coaches, Robotics Coaches, Game Design Coaches, anything explicitly "coach" in title that isn't academic coaching

 │

 ├────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

 ┤

 │ other │ Anything that doesn't fit cleanly

 │

 └────────────┴────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

 ┘

 Kill switch: ROLE_CLASSIFIER_MODE = "haiku" at top of file. Set to "pass_through" to route every contact to other — useful as a breakglass if Haiku is down.

 Decision 3 — Scrap the diocesan refactor

 The Wed 2026-04-15 drip runs tomorrow and the Thu 2026-04-16 drip runs the day after. Refactoring the live production diocesan drip mid-week carries real incident risk and zero reward — the generalized

 loader doesn't NEED to share a code path with diocesan drip to ship. scripts/diocesan_drip.py stays exactly as-is. Future migration (if it happens) is a separate plan, scheduled well after both drip runs

 complete and only when a genuine reason to converge the two paths appears.

 Decision 4 — Sidecar state, not in-file mutation

 After --create runs, sequence IDs are written to campaigns/<slug>.state.json (gitignored). The markdown file stays clean and git-diffable.

 State file schema:

 {

 "slug" : "cue_2026" ,

 "created_at" : "2026-04-14T13:45:00-05:00" ,

 "campaign_file_sha1" : "abc123..." ,

 "sequences" : {

 "admin" : { "id" : 2014 , "name" : "CUE 2026 — admin" , "created_at" : "..." },

 "teacher" : { "id" : 2015 , "name" : "CUE 2026 — teacher" , "created_at" : "..." }

 },

 "load_runs" : [

 { "started_at" : "..." , "day" : "2026-04-21" , "contacts_loaded" : 14 , "errors" : 0 }

 ]

 }

 Idempotency: --create reads sequences dict first; any variant already present (non-null id) is skipped. Re-running --create is safe.

 Drift detection: --execute compares campaign_file_sha1 against the current file. Mismatch → warn loudly ("you edited the campaign file after --create; sequences in Outreach no longer match") and require

 --force to proceed.

 Decision 5 — Three contact input modes

 The loader accepts contacts from:

 1. --contacts-csv <path> — CSV at that path. Columns: first_name, last_name, email, title, company, state, notes (minimum). Extra columns passed through to the classifier.

 2. --contacts-stdin — same CSV format piped on stdin. Enables: Steven pastes a CSV blob in Claude Code, I pipe it through the command with a heredoc or file substitution.

 3. --contacts-sheet <tab_name> — Google Sheets tab reference (uses existing GAS bridge). Reuses the diocesan drip's _lead_to_contact adapter pattern, genericized.

 v1 scope: ship CSV + stdin. Sheets adapter deferred to v2 (it's a nice-to-have, not load-bearing for the claude.ai round-trip flow).

 Decision 6 — Preflight validator, loud-and-early

 Before any --create POSTs fire, load_campaign.py runs every variant through outreach_client.validate_sequence_inputs in a dry-run pass and prints all failures at once. No partial creates. If any variant

 fails, exit non-zero and print the full failure table.

 Same for --execute : before loading contacts, check that (a) all target sequences in the state file are enabled in Outreach, (b) every contact has a valid IANA timezone via state_to_timezone , (c) the contact

 list has no obvious issues (empty email, missing state). Any failure → exit non-zero with the full failure list.

 Decision 7 — Export utility as its own tool, not a Telegram command

 outreach_client.export_sequence_for_editing(sequence_id) -> str returns a markdown blob in the same schema as Decision 1. Steven pipes the output to a new campaigns/<slug>.md file, edits in claude.ai,

 saves.

 ---

 ---

 New files:

 - tools/campaign_file.py — Campaign dataclass + markdown parser + dumper

 - tools/role_classifier.py — Haiku-backed role bucketing

 - scripts/load_campaign.py — CLI orchestrator

 - scripts/test_campaign_file.py — parser round-trip + edge-case tests

 - scripts/test_role_classifier.py — fixture-based classifier tests (uses a small mock Claude client for determinism)

 - campaigns/canary_test.md — canary fixture (3 fake variants, 5 fake contacts inline in a ## contacts section)

 - .gitignore append — campaigns/*.state.json , campaigns/*.audit.jsonl

 - tools/outreach_client.py — add export_sequence_for_editing(sequence_id) -> str

 - docs/SCOUT_CAPABILITIES.md — document new components

 - CLAUDE.md — add a "PREFLIGHT: Campaign load" checklist + update CURRENT STATE

 Files NOT modified: agent/main.py , scripts/diocesan_drip.py , tools/prospect_loader.py , tools/sequence_builder.py , agent/target_roles.py .

 ---

 Commit plan (four commits, stop-anywhere safe)

 Commit 1 — feat(campaign_file): single-file campaign markdown schema + parser

 - New: tools/campaign_file.py , scripts/test_campaign_file.py , campaigns/canary_test.md

 - Tests:

 - Round-trip — parse canary → dump → parse → equality.

 - Permissive parser — handles ### Step N — Subject: with variations in heading level ( ## , ### , #### ) and subject separator ( — , - , : ).

 - Missing frontmatter key → clear error with key name.

 - Duplicate variant roles → clear error.

 - Empty variant section → treated as no variant.

 - Verification: .venv/bin/python scripts/test_campaign_file.py must pass.

 - Stop-anywhere: this commit ships a usable parser even if nothing else ships. Unit-tested in isolation.

 Commit 2 — feat(outreach): export_sequence_for_editing — fetch sequence as claude.ai starter

 - Modified: tools/outreach_client.py — new export_sequence_for_editing(sequence_id: int) -> str . Calls get_sequence_steps , renders markdown matching Commit 1 schema, wraps in frontmatter.

 - Smoke test: export diocesan Chicago sequence (sequence "Chicago diocesan" per Rule 19 name translation) → print to stdout → eyeball output → round-trip through campaign_file.load_campaign parser (should

 parse cleanly as a one-variant campaign).

 - Verification: .venv/bin/python -c "from tools.outreach_client import Outreach Client; print(Outreach Client().export_sequence_for_editing(2013))" writes a well-formed markdown blob.

 - Stop-anywhere: shippable even without the classifier or CLI. Steven can use it manually from a Python shell.

 Commit 3 — feat(role_classifier): Haiku-backed contact role bucketing with cache

 - New: tools/role_classifier.py , scripts/test_role_classifier.py

 - Tests: fixtures for each of the 6 buckets plus 5 ambiguous edge cases ( "Director" , "Coordinator" , "Specialist" , "Lead" , "Administrator" — ambiguous alone, needs Haiku). Uses a mock Claude client for

 deterministic testing.

 - Cache: JSON at data/role_classifier_cache.json . Key = sha1(normalized title), value = bucket string + timestamp.

 - Verification:

 a. .venv/bin/python scripts/test_role_classifier.py must pass.

 b. Manual smoke: classify 10 real titles from memory/csta_roster.json (existing CSTA roster data) and eyeball the output. Accept if 9 of 10 are correct.

 - Stop-anywhere: shippable even without the CLI. Can be called from a Python shell for ad-hoc classification.

 Commit 4 — feat(load_campaign): generalized campaign loader CLI + canary smoke test + docs

 - New: scripts/load_campaign.py

 - Modified: docs/SCOUT_CAPABILITIES.md , CLAUDE.md (new PREFLIGHT: Campaign load checklist + CURRENT STATE update)

 - CLI flags:

 load_campaign.py --campaign <slug> --create # preflight + create sequences

 load_campaign.py --campaign <slug> --preview # dry-run of --execute plan

 load_campaign.py --campaign <slug> --execute # load contacts (respects drip_days)

 load_campaign.py --campaign <slug> --execute --force # bypass drift check

 load_campaign.py --contacts-csv <path> [...] # CSV contact source

 load_campaign.py --contacts-stdin [...] # piped CSV on stdin

 - Verification:

 a. .venv/bin/python scripts/load_campaign.py --campaign canary_test --preview → prints plan with 5 contacts classified into 3 buckets, routed to placeholder sequence IDs, no API calls.

 b. Integration test (manual, not automated): Steven runs --create --preview against canary_test, inspects the validator output, no writes fire.

 - Stop-anywhere: this is the terminal commit. Session 64 ends cleanly whether or not this commit lands.

 ---

 Preflight additions to CLAUDE.md (Commit 4)

 PREFLIGHT: Campaign load — triggers on any task running load_campaign.py or creating/editing files under campaigns/ .

 - Confirm the campaign markdown file parses cleanly via tools.campaign_file.load_campaign(path) — any parse error = STOP.

 - Confirm the schedule_id is in the validator allowlist (env OUTREACH_ALLOWED_SCHEDULE_IDS default {48, 50, 51, 52, 53} ).

 - Confirm every variant has a meeting link hyperlink in at least one step and codecombat.com/schools hyperlink in at least two — matches feedback_sequence_copy_rules.md .

 - Confirm drip_days are all business days, in the future, and in a reasonable range (not > 30 days out).

 - Run --create --preview first (dry-run pass through validate_sequence_inputs ); fix all failures BEFORE running --create for real.

 - After --create , open Outreach UI and activate each new sequence. --execute will refuse to load contacts into disabled sequences ( verify_sequence_active=True is the default).

 - Before --execute , confirm every contact has a populated state column that maps via state_to_timezone to a non-empty IANA tz. Missing = skip contact, never fall back (Rule 17).

 - Check sending cap headroom — the 5k/rolling-7-days per-user Outreach cap ( feedback_outreach_sending_cap_5k_weekly.md ).

 - Never bypass validate_sequence_inputs ; never pass verify_sequence_active=False without explicit reason.

 ---

 Verification plan

 Unit level (all automated, run in CI-style via .venv/bin/python ):

 .venv/bin/python scripts/test_campaign_file.py

 .venv/bin/python scripts/test_role_classifier.py

 Both must exit 0 before any commit.

 Integration level (manual smoke tests against canary):

 # Parses the canary fixture, classifies 5 fake contacts, prints the plan:

 .venv/bin/python scripts/load_campaign.py --campaign canary_test --preview

 # Runs the --create preflight (validator pass) against canary variants, no writes:

 .venv/bin/python scripts/load_campaign.py --campaign canary_test --create --preview

 Export utility smoke test:

 # Exports the Chicago diocesan sequence as a markdown starter:

 .venv/bin/python -c "from tools.outreach_client import Outreach Client; print(Outreach Client().export_sequence_for_editing(2013))" > /tmp/chicago_export.md

 .venv/bin/python -c "from tools.campaign_file import load_campaign; print(load_campaign('/tmp/chicago_export.md'))"

 Both must run without error and the parsed Campaign object must contain one variant with 5 steps.

 Live test (deferred to a later session, gated on Steven's approval):

 Real campaign — next CUE conference push or next C4 re-engagement batch. Run --create , Steven activates in Outreach UI, run --execute , verify contacts land in the right sequences via the Outreach UI. This

 is NOT a Session 64 deliverable.

 ---

 Out of scope (explicitly deferred)

 1. Diocesan drip refactor. Not until a genuine reason to converge the two paths appears, and never mid-week during a live drip.

 2. Google Sheets contact source adapter. CSV + stdin only for v1.

 3. --update command to PATCH existing sequences after copy edits. v1 warns on drift; v2 (if needed) adds PATCH.

 4. Telegram /export_sequence <name> hook. Library function ships; Telegram hook waits.

 5. Claude-powered campaign DRAFTING (vs classification). Drafting stays in claude.ai per Steven's workflow.

 6. Contact source from research engine result dict directly. The research engine writes to Sheets; the loader reads from CSV/stdin. Bridging these directly is a post-v1 convenience feature.

 7. Automated role classifier accuracy measurement. v1 eyeballs 10 real titles and accepts if 9 pass. Formal eval suite is v2.

 ---

 Session-crossover strategy

 ctx is 9% (measured — current injected marker) as of plan write. Each commit is independently shippable. Recommended execution order for Session 64:

 1. Commit 1 (campaign parser) — ~45 min (estimate). Pure data, no external deps. Safe to ship even late in the session.

 2. Commit 2 ( export_sequence_for_editing ) — ~45 min (estimate). One Outreach API call plus markdown rendering. Testable in isolation.

 3. Commit 3 (role classifier) — ~60 min (estimate). Includes Haiku smoke test against CSTA roster titles.

 4. Commit 4 (CLI + docs) — ~75-90 min (estimate). The orchestrator tying everything together. If ctx is tight, split this across Session 64 / Session 65.

 Any commit boundary is a clean resume point. The CLI in Commit 4 depends on 1+2+3; everything else is independent.

 ---

 Most important changes from v1 (changelog)

 1. Killed the diocesan refactor entirely. v1 put it in Commit 5, which would have forced production-risk changes on the day before the Wed 2026-04-15 drip and the Thu 2026-04-16 drip. No upside, real

 downside. Commit count dropped from 6 to 4.

 2. Single markdown file per campaign replaces config.yaml + variants/ directory. The directory layout forced Steven to translate claude.ai output into a new schema on every round trip. Single-file matches

 claude.ai's natural output — copy-paste-save-run. The parser becomes slightly more complex (one parser vs one YAML lib call plus one markdown lib call), but the UX win is the whole point.

 3. Role classifier is Haiku-backed, not rule-based. Rule-based on job titles hits 75-85% (estimate) and needs 150-300 lines of regex-plus-fallbacks. Haiku at temp=0 hits 92-97% (estimate) in ~50 lines at

 under $0.002/contact (estimate). v1's "simpler = rule-based" instinct was wrong — Haiku is genuinely simpler once you stop pretending title parsing is solvable deterministically.

 4. Sidecar state file for sequence_ids , not in-file mutation. v1 proposed writing sequence_ids back to config.yaml , which would dirty the markdown file after every --create run and pollute git diffs. The

 sidecar is gitignored, tracks drift via campaign file sha1, and enables clean idempotent re-runs of --create .

 5. Three contact input modes (CSV + stdin + Sheets), v1 scope is CSV + stdin. v1 said "CSV only" and ignored that Steven won't CSV-export from Sheets for every campaign. stdin is load-bearing — it's what

 makes the claude.ai round-trip work without an intermediate file.

 6. Preflight validator runs all variants BEFORE any POST. v1 didn't design this. Without it, Steven discovers validator failures one-at-a-time across multiple --create runs (because each failure aborts the

 next variant's creation). The new preflight is a single dry-run pass through validate_sequence_inputs that prints all failures at once.

 7. Drift detection via campaign file sha1. v1 had no guard against Steven editing the campaign file after --create ran. The state file stores the sha1 of the markdown file at --create time; --execute

 compares and warns loudly on mismatch. --force overrides for edge cases.

 8. Renamed sequence_drip.py → load_campaign.py . "Drip" implies time-spaced sends. Most campaigns are one-day bulk loads. The generalization is a campaign loader, not a drip.

 9. Bucketed classifier deferred to v2: Claude-powered ambiguity fallback, role reclassification UI, and accuracy eval suite. v1 is the minimum viable classifier — Haiku with a prompt and a cache.

 10. Dropped tools/campaign_config.py from the plan. v1 had it as a separate module; it's now merged into tools/campaign_file.py as a single parser-plus-dataclass module. One module, one responsibility

 (parsing the campaign markdown file into typed data).

 1. tools/prospect_loader.py:56-74 — Contact dataclass contract (field names used by build_load_plan ).

 2. tools/prospect_loader.py:117 — build_load_plan signature ( sequence_id_for callback shape, tags_for callback shape, days type).

 3. tools/prospect_loader.py:259 — execute_load_plan signature ( state_path , audit_path , sleep_seconds tuple format).

 4. tools/outreach_client.py:814 — validate_sequence_inputs return shape ( {passed, failures, warnings} ).

 5. tools/outreach_client.py — get_sequence_steps signature (what shape the steps come back in, for export_sequence_for_editing ).

 6. tools/outreach_client.py — create_sequence signature (what fields are required, what the step dict should look like).

 7. agent/target_roles.py:333 — is_relevant_role(title) exact behavior (for the classifier pre-filter).

 8. scripts/diocesan_drip.py:116-142 — _lead_to_contact adapter pattern (reference for the CSV → Contact conversion, not for copying).

 9. agent/claude_brain.py — Anthropic client instantiation pattern (what client the classifier should import or instantiate).

 ⏺ Three agents reported. Key findings: prospect_loader + diocesan_drip are already 90% strategy-agnostic (sample — from agent's line-by-line map); role classification is truly greenfield; handler confirmed never

 calls create_sequence ; the loader pattern has been hand-coded three times (CUE, C4, webinar) before the S61 library promotion.

 ⏺ Updated plan

 ⎿  /plan to preview

 ⎿  Tool use rejected

 ⏺ Rejecting v1 and rebuilding. Stopping to think through the whole plan as a system before touching anything.

 ---

 What I got wrong in v1 (the foundation)

 1. Wrong schema shape for the claude.ai round-trip. I proposed campaigns/<slug>/config.yaml + variants/<role>.md + a YAML loader. That schema forces Steven to translate claude.ai's natural output (one

 conversational markdown blob) into a multi-file directory structure on every round trip. Friction at the worst point in the workflow. The correct shape is a single markdown file per campaign with frontmatter +

 ## variant: <role> sections, matching what claude.ai naturally produces. Copy-paste-save-run.

 2. Wrong choice for the role classifier. I proposed a rule-based classifier using target_roles.py tier lists as seed data. Rule-based classifiers on job titles cap out at maybe 75-85% accuracy (estimate —

 ambiguous-title cases like "Director", "Coordinator", "Specialist" break deterministic rules) and require 150-300 lines of regex-plus-fallbacks to get there. Haiku at temp=0 will hit 92-97% (estimate) in 50

 lines of code for a per-contact cost under a penny (estimate — based on Haiku pricing and typical title length). Rule-based isn't "simpler" — it's a maintenance burden disguised as simplicity.

 3. Including the diocesan refactor was a production-risk blunder. The Wed 2026-04-15 drip runs tomorrow and the Thu 2026-04-16 drip runs the day after (measured — from CLAUDE.md CURRENT STATE). Refactoring

 live production machinery mid-week, when the refactor is cosmetic and not required for any new campaign, is exactly the sort of thing that turns a clean plan into a Monday-morning incident. Kill it from the

 plan entirely. Diocesan drip stays untouched. The new loader lives alongside it. When diocesan next needs a change, that's when the migration happens.

 4. Too many commits, overly ceremonial. Six commits with a schema-only commit, a separate docs commit, and a refactor commit that was already being cut. Collapse to four : parser + schema, role classifier,

 export utility, CLI orchestrator. Docs update rides on the last commit. No standalone "schema" commit — the parser test IS the schema validation.

 5. Misnamed the script. sequence_drip.py implies time-spaced sends. Most campaigns are one-day bulk loads. The generalization is a campaign loader , not a drip. Rename to scripts/load_campaign.py .

 6. Punted the hardest UX question. How does Steven get research-engine contacts INTO a campaign file? I handwaved "CSV". Reality: Steven is not going to CSV-export from Sheets for every campaign. The loader

 needs to accept stdin (paste) , path to a CSV , OR a Google Sheets tab reference . stdin is load-bearing — it's what makes the claude.ai round-trip work without an intermediate file.

 7. Sidecar state mishandled. I wrote "store sequence_ids back into config.yaml" — which means mutating a source-of-truth file after --create runs, which is fragile and breaks git diffs. Correct pattern:

 sidecar state file at campaigns/<slug>.state.json (gitignored), separate from the campaign markdown.

 8. Missing: pre-flight validator that surfaces ALL failures at once. outreach_client.validate_sequence_inputs returns failures one variant at a time. If Steven's campaign file has 5 variants and 4 have

 validator failures, he discovers them one-at-a-time across four --create runs. The loader needs to dry-run every variant through validate_sequence_inputs before any POST hits Outreach , and print all failures

 in one output. This is a required UX primitive, not a nice-to-have.

 9. Missing: idempotency on --create . Running it twice shouldn't produce duplicate sequences. The sidecar state file solves this — skip variants whose sequence_id is already set.

 10. Missing: what happens when Steven edits copy mid-campaign. After --create runs, the sequence is live in Outreach. Editing campaigns/cue_2026.md doesn't update Outreach. v1 needs to warn on this (compare

 state-file snapshot hash to current file hash) and leave --update as a v2 feature.

 ⏺ Updated plan

 ⎿  /plan to preview

 ⏺ User approved Claude's plan

 ⎿  Plan saved to: ~/.claude/plans/luminous-honking-cook.md · /plan to edit

 Plan — Generalized Campaign Loader with Role Segmentation and Claude.ai Round-Trip

 Rev 2 — rebuilt from scratch after v1 pressure-test (2026-04-14 S64 plan mode)

 ---

 Context

 Steven's production workflow for campaign loads is:

 1. Draft copy and subjects (starter comes from either the handler auto-draft at agent/main.py:482 or an existing Outreach sequence copied out as a starter).

 2. Iterate the copy in claude.ai — that's the preferred drafting surface because the editing UX beats Claude Code for prose work.

 3. Drop contacts into Claude Code.

 4. Classify each contact by role/avatar (admin / curriculum / IT / teacher / coach / other).

 5. Produce role-variant copy sets (one per bucket).

 6. Call outreach_client.create_sequence per variant to create real live sequences in Outreach.

 7. Steven verifies and activates each sequence manually in the Outreach UI (Rule 15 — all sequences are drafts until Steven signs off).

 8. Claude Code loads the right contacts into the right sequences, with IANA timezone enforcement and same-district stagger (via execute_load_plan sleep_seconds, already built).

 This pattern has been hand-coded three times in Scout history: S38 CUE conference loader ( scripts/enrich_cue_leads.py , 2,422 lines, commit 7d9a539 ), S43 C4 cold license request loader

 ( scripts/create_c4_sequences.py + enrich_c4_pass2.py , 1,622 lines combined, commit 353b1f0 ), and S44 webinar registrant tagging ( aabdb0c ). The S61 library promotion ( fdf6920 ) built create_prospect /

 find_prospect_by_email / add_prospect_to_sequence / build_load_plan / execute_load_plan but stopped short of the orchestration layer — the diocesan drip is the only committed campaign runner, and it's

 diocesan-specific at the CLI boundary (6 hardcoded sequence IDs 2008-2013, hardcoded DRIP_DAYS, hardcoded MAILBOX_ID).

 Intended outcome: ship a strategy-agnostic campaign loader so the next CUE / C4 / webinar / bond-trigger push uses library code instead of spawning a 4th ephemeral script. Rule 18 is the driving constraint

 — "promote ephemeral patterns before using them."

 Rule compliance:

 - Rule 1 — plan-mode required (this file).

 - Rule 7 — kill switches per component.

 - Rule 8 — one commit per feature.

 - Rule 11 — loader is a sync CLI script, not async; no time.sleep /async conflict.

 - Rule 15 — loader creates sequences but never activates; Steven activates manually in Outreach UI.

 - Rule 17 — timezone enforcement already handled at validate_prospect_inputs boundary.

 - Rule 18 — this plan IS the promotion; the promoted pattern is a single entry point ( scripts/load_campaign.py ) that future work reuses.

 - Rule 19 — loader output translates all Outreach IDs to human names before printing; no numeric IDs in stdout.

 ---

 Architectural decisions (locked)

 Decision 1 — Single markdown file per campaign, not a directory

 A campaign lives at campaigns/<slug>.md — one file, YAML frontmatter for metadata, ## variant: <role> H2 sections for each role's copy.

 Why: claude.ai produces conversational markdown. Directory-per-campaign with config.yaml + variants/*.md forces Steven to translate claude.ai output into a new schema on every round trip. Single-file lets

 him copy-paste-save.

 Schema (concrete, committed):

 ---

 campaign _name: "CUE 2026"

 campaign_ slug: "cue _2026"

 schedule_ id: 50 # C4 Tue-Thu

 drip _days:

 - 2026-04-21

 - 2026-04-22

 - 2026-04-23

 tag_ template: "cue-2026-{role}"

 sleep _seconds_ min: 60 # per-POST stagger, seconds

 sleep _seconds_ max: 180

 ---

 ## variant: admin

 target _role_ label: "Superintendent / Principal"

 num _steps: 5

 ### Step 1 — Subject: Quick note on your CS rollout at {{company}}

 Hi {{first_ name}},

 ...

 ### Step 2 — Subject: Re: Quick note on your CS rollout at {{company}}

 ...

 ## variant: teacher

 target _role_ label: "CS Teacher / Math Teacher"

 num _steps: 5

 ### Step 1 — Subject: ...

 ...

 What's NOT in the frontmatter: mailbox_id and owner_id . Both are hardcoded to 11 in outreach_client (Steven is the only user). Putting them in the schema adds surface area Steven will forget to set.

 Parser contract ( tools/campaign_file.py ):

 @dataclass

 class CampaignStep:

 subject: str

 body_html: str # parsed from the markdown body — plain text acceptable

 interval_seconds: int # default from a constant, not in schema

 @dataclass

 class CampaignVariant:

 role: str # "admin" | "curriculum" | "it" | "teacher" | "coach" | "other"

 target_role_label: str

 num_steps: int

 steps: list [CampaignStep]

 @dataclass

 class Campaign:

 name: str

 slug: str

 schedule_id: int

 drip_days: list [date]

 tag_template: str

 sleep_seconds: tuple [ int , int ]

 variants: dict [ str , CampaignVariant]

 def load_campaign(path: Path) -> Campaign:

 """Parse campaigns/<slug>.md into a Campaign. Permissive markdown parser."""

 def dump_campaign(campaign: Campaign) -> str :

 """Inverse — for export_sequence_for_editing round-trip."""

 Decision 2 — Claude Haiku for role classification, not rule-based

 tools/role_classifier.py calls Haiku at temp=0 with a six-bucket prompt per contact, results cached by SHA1 of the normalized title to avoid re-calling on reruns.

 Why: rule-based on job titles caps at 75-85% (estimate — ambiguous-title failure modes like "Director" / "Coordinator" / "Specialist"). Haiku at temp=0 should hit 92-97% (estimate). Cost estimate: under

 $0.002 per contact (estimate — Haiku input ~400 tokens, output ~20 tokens, at Haiku 4.5 pricing). A 1,119-contact C4 batch costs under $3 (estimate) — well inside the $25/week research budget.

 Interface:

 def classify_contact_role(contact: dict , *, cache_path: Path | None = None ) -> str :

 """

 Return one of: admin, curriculum, it, teacher, coach, other.

 Never raises — unclassifiable contacts return 'other'.

 """

 Pre-filter: call agent.target_roles.is_relevant_role(title) first. If False → return other without calling Claude. Saves cost on trades / IT_INFRA rows.

 Bucket definitions (in the Haiku prompt, not in code):

 ┌────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

 ┐

 │ Bucket │ Includes

 │

 ├────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

 ┤

 │ admin │ Superintendents, Principals, Assistant Principals, Asst Supts, Executive Directors, Heads of School, District Office senior leadership

 │

 ├────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

 ┤

 │ curriculum │ Curriculum Directors, C&I, Academic Coaches, Instructional Coaches, Assessment Coordinators, CS Curriculum Leads

 │

 ├────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

 ┤

 │ it │ Directors of Technology, CIOs, CTOs, EdTech Coordinators, Instructional Technology Coordinators (explicitly NOT help desk / sysadmin — those fall through to other via is_relevant_role

 │

 │ │ pre-filter)

 │

 ├────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

 ┤

 │ teacher │ CS Teachers, Math Teachers, Algebra Teachers, STEM Teachers, Robotics Teachers, classroom-level roles

 │

 ├────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

 ┤

 │ coach │ Athletic Coaches, Esports Coaches, Robotics Coaches, Game Design Coaches, anything explicitly "coach" in title that isn't academic coaching

 │

 ├────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

 ┤

 │ other │ Anything that doesn't fit cleanly

 │

 └────────────┴────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

 ┘

 Kill switch: ROLE_CLASSIFIER_MODE = "haiku" at top of file. Set to "pass_through" to route every contact to other — useful as a breakglass if Haiku is down.

 Decision 3 — Scrap the diocesan refactor

 The Wed 2026-04-15 drip runs tomorrow and the Thu 2026-04-16 drip runs the day after. Refactoring the live production diocesan drip mid-week carries real incident risk and zero reward — the generalized

 loader doesn't NEED to share a code path with diocesan drip to ship. scripts/diocesan_drip.py stays exactly as-is. Future migration (if it happens) is a separate plan, scheduled well after both drip runs

 complete and only when a genuine reason to converge the two paths appears.

 Decision 4 — Sidecar state, not in-file mutation

 After --create runs, sequence IDs are written to campaigns/<slug>.state.json (gitignored). The markdown file stays clean and git-diffable.

 State file schema:

 {

 "slug" : "cue_2026" ,

 "created_at" : "2026-04-14T13:45:00-05:00" ,

 "campaign_file_sha1" : "abc123..." ,

 "sequences" : {

 "admin" : { "id" : 2014 , "name" : "CUE 2026 — admin" , "created_at" : "..." },

 "teacher" : { "id" : 2015 , "name" : "CUE 2026 — teacher" , "created_at" : "..." }

 },

 "load_runs" : [

 { "started_at" : "..." , "day" : "2026-04-21" , "contacts_loaded" : 14 , "errors" : 0 }

 ]

 }

 Idempotency: --create reads sequences dict first; any variant already present (non-null id) is skipped. Re-running --create is safe.

 Drift detection: --execute compares campaign_file_sha1 against the current file. Mismatch → warn loudly ("you edited the campaign file after --create; sequences in Outreach no longer match") and require

 --force to proceed.

 Decision 5 — Three contact input modes

 The loader accepts contacts from:

 1. --contacts-csv <path> — CSV at that path. Columns: first_name, last_name, email, title, company, state, notes (minimum). Extra columns passed through to the classifier.

 2. --contacts-stdin — same CSV format piped on stdin. Enables: Steven pastes a CSV blob in Claude Code, I pipe it through the command with a heredoc or file substitution.

 3. --contacts-sheet <tab_name> — Google Sheets tab reference (uses existing GAS bridge). Reuses the diocesan drip's _lead_to_contact adapter pattern, genericized.

 v1 scope: ship CSV + stdin. Sheets adapter deferred to v2 (it's a nice-to-have, not load-bearing for the claude.ai round-trip flow).

 Decision 6 — Preflight validator, loud-and-early

 Before any --create POSTs fire, load_campaign.py runs every variant through outreach_client.validate_sequence_inputs in a dry-run pass and prints all failures at once. No partial creates. If any variant

 fails, exit non-zero and print the full failure table.

 Same for --execute : before loading contacts, check that (a) all target sequences in the state file are enabled in Outreach, (b) every contact has a valid IANA timezone via state_to_timezone , (c) the contact

 list has no obvious issues (empty email, missing state). Any failure → exit non-zero with the full failure list.

 Decision 7 — Export utility as its own tool, not a Telegram command

 outreach_client.export_sequence_for_editing(sequence_id) -> str returns a markdown blob in the same schema as Decision 1. Steven pipes the output to a new campaigns/<slug>.md file, edits in claude.ai,

 saves.

 ---

 ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────┬─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐

 │ Piece │ Location │ Role in plan │

 ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

 │ Contact dataclass │ tools/prospect_loader.py:56-74 │ Target shape for CSV/stdin → loader boundary. diocese_or_group field = generic campaign_group label │

 │ │ │ (no rename). │

 ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

 │ build_load_plan(...) │ tools/prospect_loader.py:117 │ Called from load_campaign --execute . Callbacks: sequence_id_for routes by classified role, tags_for │

 │ │ │ applies tag_template , days = campaign drip_days . │

 ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

 │ execute_load_plan(...) │ tools/prospect_loader.py:259 │ Called from load_campaign --execute . State path = campaigns/<slug>.state.json , audit path = │

 │ │ │ campaigns/<slug>.audit.jsonl . │

 ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

 │ create_sequence(...) │ tools/outreach_client.py │ Called from load_campaign --create . Validator auto-runs. │

 ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

 │ validate_sequence_inputs(...) │ tools/outreach_client.py:814 │ Called manually in the --create preflight pass to surface all failures before any POST. │

 ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

 │ get_sequence_steps(...) │ tools/outreach_client.py │ Called from export_sequence_for_editing to pull an existing sequence out. │

 ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

 │ create_prospect / find_prospect_by_email / add_prospect_to_sequence │ tools/outreach_client.py │ All called from inside execute_load_plan . Zero new wiring. │

 │ / validate_prospect_inputs │ │ │

 ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

 │ state_to_timezone(state) │ tools/timezone_lookup.py │ Called in contact hydration; skip contacts with missing or non-US state. │

 ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

 │ is_relevant_role(title) │ agent/target_roles.py:333 │ Pre-filter before Haiku classification. │

 └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┴────────────────────────────────┴─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

 ---

 New files:

 - tools/campaign_file.py — Campaign dataclass + markdown parser + dumper

 - tools/role_classifier.py — Haiku-backed role bucketing

 - scripts/load_campaign.py — CLI orchestrator

 - scripts/test_campaign_file.py — parser round-trip + edge-case tests

 - scripts/test_role_classifier.py — fixture-based classifier tests (uses a small mock Claude client for determinism)

 - campaigns/canary_test.md — canary fixture (3 fake variants, 5 fake contacts inline in a ## contacts section)

 - .gitignore append — campaigns/*.state.json , campaigns/*.audit.jsonl

 - tools/outreach_client.py — add export_sequence_for_editing(sequence_id) -> str

 - docs/SCOUT_CAPABILITIES.md — document new components

 - CLAUDE.md — add a "PREFLIGHT: Campaign load" checklist + update CURRENT STATE

 Files NOT modified: agent/main.py , scripts/diocesan_drip.py , tools/prospect_loader.py , tools/sequence_builder.py , agent/target_roles.py .

 ---

 Commit plan (four commits, stop-anywhere safe)

 Commit 1 — feat(campaign_file): single-file campaign markdown schema + parser

 - New: tools/campaign_file.py , scripts/test_campaign_file.py , campaigns/canary_test.md

 - Tests:

 - Round-trip — parse canary → dump → parse → equality.

 - Permissive parser — handles ### Step N — Subject: with variations in heading level ( ## , ### , #### ) and subject separator ( — , - , : ).

 - Missing frontmatter key → clear error with key name.

 - Duplicate variant roles → clear error.

 - Empty variant section → treated as no variant.

 - Verification: .venv/bin/python scripts/test_campaign_file.py must pass.

 - Stop-anywhere: this commit ships a usable parser even if nothing else ships. Unit-tested in isolation.

 Commit 2 — feat(outreach): export_sequence_for_editing — fetch sequence as claude.ai starter

 - Modified: tools/outreach_client.py — new export_sequence_for_editing(sequence_id: int) -> str . Calls get_sequence_steps , renders markdown matching Commit 1 schema, wraps in frontmatter.

 - Smoke test: export diocesan Chicago sequence (sequence "Chicago diocesan" per Rule 19 name translation) → print to stdout → eyeball output → round-trip through campaign_file.load_campaign parser (should

 parse cleanly as a one-variant campaign).

 - Verification: .venv/bin/python -c "from tools.outreach_client import Outreach Client; print(Outreach Client().export_sequence_for_editing(2013))" writes a well-formed markdown blob.

 - Stop-anywhere: shippable even without the classifier or CLI. Steven can use it manually from a Python shell.

 Commit 3 — feat(role_classifier): Haiku-backed contact role bucketing with cache

 - New: tools/role_classifier.py , scripts/test_role_classifier.py

 - Tests: fixtures for each of the 6 buckets plus 5 ambiguous edge cases ( "Director" , "Coordinator" , "Specialist" , "Lead" , "Administrator" — ambiguous alone, needs Haiku). Uses a mock Claude client for

 deterministic testing.

 - Cache: JSON at data/role_classifier_cache.json . Key = sha1(normalized title), value = bucket string + timestamp.

 - Verification:

 a. .venv/bin/python scripts/test_role_classifier.py must pass.

 b. Manual smoke: classify 10 real titles from memory/csta_roster.json (existing CSTA roster data) and eyeball the output. Accept if 9 of 10 are correct.

 - Stop-anywhere: shippable even without the CLI. Can be called from a Python shell for ad-hoc classification.

 Commit 4 — feat(load_campaign): generalized campaign loader CLI + canary smoke test + docs

 - New: scripts/load_campaign.py

 - Modified: docs/SCOUT_CAPABILITIES.md , CLAUDE.md (new PREFLIGHT: Campaign load checklist + CURRENT STATE update)

 - CLI flags:

 load_campaign.py --campaign <slug> --create # preflight + create sequences

 load_campaign.py --campaign <slug> --preview # dry-run of --execute plan

 load_campaign.py --campaign <slug> --execute # load contacts (respects drip_days)

 load_campaign.py --campaign <slug> --execute --force # bypass drift check

 load_campaign.py --contacts-csv <path> [...] # CSV contact source

 load_campaign.py --contacts-stdin [...] # piped CSV on stdin

 - Verification:

 a. .venv/bin/python scripts/load_campaign.py --campaign canary_test --preview → prints plan with 5 contacts classified into 3 buckets, routed to placeholder sequence IDs, no API calls.

 b. Integration test (manual, not automated): Steven runs --create --preview against canary_test, inspects the validator output, no writes fire.

 - Stop-anywhere: this is the terminal commit. Session 64 ends cleanly whether or not this commit lands.

 ---

 Preflight additions to CLAUDE.md (Commit 4)

 PREFLIGHT: Campaign load — triggers on any task running load_campaign.py or creating/editing files under campaigns/ .

 - Confirm the campaign markdown file parses cleanly via tools.campaign_file.load_campaign(path) — any parse error = STOP.

 - Confirm the schedule_id is in the validator allowlist (env OUTREACH_ALLOWED_SCHEDULE_IDS default {48, 50, 51, 52, 53} ).

 - Confirm every variant has a meeting link hyperlink in at least one step and codecombat.com/schools hyperlink in at least two — matches feedback_sequence_copy_rules.md .

 - Confirm drip_days are all business days, in the future, and in a reasonable range (not > 30 days out).

 - Run --create --preview first (dry-run pass through validate_sequence_inputs ); fix all failures BEFORE running --create for real.

 - After --create , open Outreach UI and activate each new sequence. --execute will refuse to load contacts into disabled sequences ( verify_sequence_active=True is the default).

 - Before --execute , confirm every contact has a populated state column that maps via state_to_timezone to a non-empty IANA tz. Missing = skip contact, never fall back (Rule 17).

 - Check sending cap headroom — the 5k/rolling-7-days per-user Outreach cap ( feedback_outreach_sending_cap_5k_weekly.md ).

 - Never bypass validate_sequence_inputs ; never pass verify_sequence_active=False without explicit reason.

 ---

 Verification plan

 Unit level (all automated, run in CI-style via .venv/bin/python ):

 .venv/bin/python scripts/test_campaign_file.py

 .venv/bin/python scripts/test_role_classifier.py

 Both must exit 0 before any commit.

 Integration level (manual smoke tests against canary):

 # Parses the canary fixture, classifies 5 fake contacts, prints the plan:

 .venv/bin/python scripts/load_campaign.py --campaign canary_test --preview

 # Runs the --create preflight (validator pass) against canary variants, no writes:

 .venv/bin/python scripts/load_campaign.py --campaign canary_test --create --preview

 Export utility smoke test:

 # Exports the Chicago diocesan sequence as a markdown starter:

 .venv/bin/python -c "from tools.outreach_client import Outreach Client; print(Outreach Client().export_sequence_for_editing(2013))" > /tmp/chicago_export.md

 .venv/bin/python -c "from tools.campaign_file import load_campaign; print(load_campaign('/tmp/chicago_export.md'))"

 Both must run without error and the parsed Campaign object must contain one variant with 5 steps.

 Live test (deferred to a later session, gated on Steven's approval):

 Real campaign — next CUE conference push or next C4 re-engagement batch. Run --create , Steven activates in Outreach UI, run --execute , verify contacts land in the right sequences via the Outreach UI. This

 is NOT a Session 64 deliverable.

 ---

 Out of scope (explicitly deferred)

 1. Diocesan drip refactor. Not until a genuine reason to converge the two paths appears, and never mid-week during a live drip.

 2. Google Sheets contact source adapter. CSV + stdin only for v1.

 3. --update command to PATCH existing sequences after copy edits. v1 warns on drift; v2 (if needed) adds PATCH.

 4. Telegram /export_sequence <name> hook. Library function ships; Telegram hook waits.

 5. Claude-powered campaign DRAFTING (vs classification). Drafting stays in claude.ai per Steven's workflow.

 6. Contact source from research engine result dict directly. The research engine writes to Sheets; the loader reads from CSV/stdin. Bridging these directly is a post-v1 convenience feature.

 7. Automated role classifier accuracy measurement. v1 eyeballs 10 real titles and accepts if 9 pass. Formal eval suite is v2.

 ---

 Session-crossover strategy

 ctx is 9% (measured — current injected marker) as of plan write. Each commit is independently shippable. Recommended execution order for Session 64:

 1. Commit 1 (campaign parser) — ~45 min (estimate). Pure data, no external deps. Safe to ship even late in the session.

 2. Commit 2 ( export_sequence_for_editing ) — ~45 min (estimate). One Outreach API call plus markdown rendering. Testable in isolation.

 3. Commit 3 (role classifier) — ~60 min (estimate). Includes Haiku smoke test against CSTA roster titles.

 4. Commit 4 (CLI + docs) — ~75-90 min (estimate). The orchestrator tying everything together. If ctx is tight, split this across Session 64 / Session 65.

 Any commit boundary is a clean resume point. The CLI in Commit 4 depends on 1+2+3; everything else is independent.

 ---

 Most important changes from v1 (changelog)

 1. Killed the diocesan refactor entirely. v1 put it in Commit 5, which would have forced production-risk changes on the day before the Wed 2026-04-15 drip and the Thu 2026-04-16 drip. No upside, real

 downside. Commit count dropped from 6 to 4.

 2. Single markdown file per campaign replaces config.yaml + variants/ directory. The directory layout forced Steven to translate claude.ai output into a new schema on every round trip. Single-file matches

 claude.ai's natural output — copy-paste-save-run. The parser becomes slightly more complex (one parser vs one YAML lib call plus one markdown lib call), but the UX win is the whole point.

 3. Role classifier is Haiku-backed, not rule-based. Rule-based on job titles hits 75-85% (estimate) and needs 150-300 lines of regex-plus-fallbacks. Haiku at temp=0 hits 92-97% (estimate) in ~50 lines at

 under $0.002/contact (estimate). v1's "simpler = rule-based" instinct was wrong — Haiku is genuinely simpler once you stop pretending title parsing is solvable deterministically.

 4. Sidecar state file for sequence_ids , not in-file mutation. v1 proposed writing sequence_ids back to config.yaml , which would dirty the markdown file after every --create run and pollute git diffs. The

 sidecar is gitignored, tracks drift via campaign file sha1, and enables clean idempotent re-runs of --create .

 5. Three contact input modes (CSV + stdin + Sheets), v1 scope is CSV + stdin. v1 said "CSV only" and ignored that Steven won't CSV-export from Sheets for every campaign. stdin is load-bearing — it's what

 makes the claude.ai round-trip work without an intermediate file.

 6. Preflight validator runs all variants BEFORE any POST. v1 didn't design this. Without it, Steven discovers validator failures one-at-a-time across multiple --create runs (because each failure aborts the

 next variant's creation). The new preflight is a single dry-run pass through validate_sequence_inputs that prints all failures at once.

 7. Drift detection via campaign file sha1. v1 had no guard against Steven editing the campaign file after --create ran. The state file stores the sha1 of the markdown file at --create time; --execute

 compares and warns loudly on mismatch. --force overrides for edge cases.

 8. Renamed sequence_drip.py → load_campaign.py . "Drip" implies time-spaced sends. Most campaigns are one-day bulk loads. The generalization is a campaign loader, not a drip.

 9. Bucketed classifier deferred to v2: Claude-powered ambiguity fallback, role reclassification UI, and accuracy eval suite. v1 is the minimum viable classifier — Haiku with a prompt and a cache.

 10. Dropped tools/campaign_config.py from the plan. v1 had it as a separate module; it's now merged into tools/campaign_file.py as a single parser-plus-dataclass module. One module, one responsibility

 (parsing the campaign markdown file into typed data).

 ---

 Critical files to read before starting implementation

 1. tools/prospect_loader.py:56-74 — Contact dataclass contract (field names used by build_load_plan ).

 2. tools/prospect_loader.py:117 — build_load_plan signature ( sequence_id_for callback shape, tags_for callback shape, days type).

 3. tools/prospect_loader.py:259 — execute_load_plan signature ( state_path , audit_path , sleep_seconds tuple format).

 4. tools/outreach_client.py:814 — validate_sequence_inputs return shape ( {passed, failures, warnings} ).

 5. tools/outreach_client.py — get_sequence_steps signature (what shape the steps come back in, for export_sequence_for_editing ).

 6. tools/outreach_client.py — create_sequence signature (what fields are required, what the step dict should look like).

 7. agent/target_roles.py:333 — is_relevant_role(title) exact behavior (for the classifier pre-filter).

 8. scripts/diocesan_drip.py:116-142 — _lead_to_contact adapter pattern (reference for the CSV → Contact conversion, not for copying).

 9. agent/claude_brain.py — Anthropic client instantiation pattern (what client the classifier should import or instantiate).

 4 load_campaign.py CLI + docs

 ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── campaign-loader-role-segmentation ──

 ⎿   Added 99 lines
 2049 lines.append( "" ) # blank line between steps
 2051 return "\n" .join(lines)
 2052 +
 2053 +
 2054 + def export_sequence_for_editing (
 2055 + sequence_id: int | str ,
 2056 + *,
 2057 + role: str = "other" ,
 2058 + target_role_label: str = "" ,
 2059 + slug_override: str = "" ,

 2060 + ) -> str :

 2061 + """

 2062 + Fetch an existing Outreach sequence and render it as a

 2063 + campaigns/<slug>.md starter — same markdown schema as

 2064 + tools/campaign_file.py parses.

 2065 +

 2066 + Purpose: Steven pipes this output to a file, opens it in claude.ai,

 2067 + iterates on the copy as a starter for a new campaign, then saves the

 2068 + result back into campaigns/ for load_campaign.py to ingest.

 2069 +

 2070 + v1 caveats:

 2071 + - Only produces a single-variant campaign. Steven can add more

 2072 + ` ## variant: <role>` sections manually after pasting into claude.ai.

 2073 + - Step bodies are copied as - is (typically HTML from Outreach). The

 2074 + parser stores them opaquely, so round -trip is lossless; claude.ai

 2075 + can rewrite the HTML if Steven wants a cleaner starter.

 2076 + - drip_days defaults to a single placeholder (today). Steven sets

 2077 + the real dates when finalizing the new campaign file.

 2078 + """

 2079 + from datetime import date as _date

 2080 +

 2081 + # Local import to avoid a hard dep at module import time.

 2082 + from tools.campaign_file import (

 2083 + Campaign,

 2084 + CampaignStep,

 2085 + CampaignVariant,

 2086 + SECONDS_PER_DAY,

 2087 + VALID_ROLES,

 2088 + dump_campaign,

 2089 + )

 2090 +

 2091 + role = (role or "other" ).lower()

 2092 + if role not in VALID_ROLES:

 2093 + raise ValueError( f"role must be one of { sorted (VALID_ROLES)} , got {role!r} " )

 2094 +

 2095 + seq_info = export_sequence(sequence_id)

 2096 +

 2097 + name = seq_info.get( "name" ) or f"Exported Sequence {sequence_id} "

 2098 + if slug_override:

 2099 + slug = slug_override

 2100 + else :

 2101 + slug = "" .join(

 2102 + c if c.isalnum() else "_" for c in name.lower()

 2103 + ).strip( "_" ) or f"sequence_ {sequence_id} "

 2104 +

 2105 + schedule_id_raw = seq_info.get( "schedule_id" )

 2106 + schedule_id = int (schedule_id_raw) if schedule_id_raw else 0

 2107 +

 2108 + raw_steps = seq_info.get( "steps" ) or []

 2109 + campaign_steps: list [CampaignStep] = []

 2110 + for i, s in enumerate (raw_steps, start= 1 ):

 2111 + subject = (s.get( "subject" ) or s.get( "name" ) or f"Step {i} " ).strip()

 2112 + body = s.get( "body_html" ) or s.get( "body_text" ) or ""

 2113 + interval_seconds = int (s.get( "interval" ) or 0 )

 2114 + campaign_steps.append(

 2115 + CampaignStep(

 2116 + step_number=i,

 2117 + subject=subject,

 2118 + body=body,

 2119 + interval_seconds=interval_seconds,

 2120 + )

 2121 + )

 2122 +

 2123 + if not campaign_steps:

 2124 + raise ValueError(

 2125 + f"sequence {sequence_id} has no steps with fetched template content"

 2126 + )

 2127 +

 2128 + step_intervals_days = [

 2129 + max ( 0 , round (s.interval_seconds / SECONDS_PER_DAY)) for s in campaign_steps

 2130 + ]

 2131 +

 2132 + variant = CampaignVariant(

 2133 + role=role,

 2134 + target_role_label=target_role_label or "TBD — fill in when editing" ,

 2135 + num_steps= len (campaign_steps),

 2136 + steps=campaign_steps,

 2137 + )

 2138 +

 2139 + campaign = Campaign(

 2140 + name=name,

 2141 + slug=slug,

 2142 + schedule_id=schedule_id,

 2143 + drip_days=[_date.today()],

 2144 + tag_template= f" {slug} -{{role}}" ,

 2145 + sleep_seconds=( 60 , 180 ),

 2146 + step_intervals_days=step_intervals_days,

 2147 + variants={role: variant},

 2148 + )

 2149 +

 2150 + return dump_campaign(campaign)
