 67 + ## `tools/campaign_file.py` — *new S64*

 68 +

 69 + Single-file campaign markdown schema for multi-variant campaigns. Lives at `campaigns/<slug>.md` with YAML frontmatter + `## variant: <role>` sections. Used by `scripts/load_campaign.py` as the

 + claude.ai round-trip surface: Steven drafts copy in claude.ai, pastes into the file, loader ingests.

 70 +

 71 + - `Campaign`, `Campaign Variant`, `Campaign Step` dataclasses

 72 + - `load_campaign(path)` / `parse_campaign(text)` — permissive markdown parser (handles H2/H3/H4 step headings, `—` / `-` / `:` separators)

 73 + - `dump_campaign(campaign)` — inverse emitter, round-trip safe

 74 + - `VALID_ROLES` — `admin | curriculum | it | teacher | coach | other`

 75 + - `DEFAULT_STEP_INTERVALS_DAYS = [0, 5, 6, 7, 8]` — diocesan-matching cadence

 76 +

 77 + Tests: `scripts/test_campaign_file.py` (10 cases, round-trip + edge cases).

 78 +

 79 + ---

 80 +

 81 + ## `tools/role_classifier.py` — *new S64*

 82 +

 83 + Haiku-backed contact role bucketing. Classifies a K-12 contact (primarily by title) into one of six buckets via Claude Haiku at `temp=0`. Used by `scripts/load_campaign.py` to route contacts to

 + role-variant sequences in multi-variant campaigns.

 84 +

 85 + - `classify_contact_role(contact, *, client=None, cache_path=None)` — returns one of `admin | curriculum | it | teacher | coach | other`. Never raises.

 86 + - Pre-filter: `agent.target_roles.is_relevant_role` drops IT infra + irrelevant CTE trades to `other` without a Claude call.

 87 + - Cache: sha1(normalized title) → bucket, at `data/role_classifier_cache.json` (gitignored, reused across runs).

 88 + - Kill switch: `ROLE_CLASSIFIER_MODE=pass_through` env var routes every contact to `other`.

 89 +

 90 + S64 live smoke test: 14/14 real K-12 titles classified correctly. Tests: `scripts/test_role_classifier.py` (11 cases using mock Anthropic client).

 91 +

 92 + ---

 93 +

 94 + ## `scripts/load_campaign.py` — *new S64*

 95 +

 96 + Generalized campaign loader CLI. Strategy-agnostic replacement for the pattern hand-coded three times (S38 CUE, S43 C4, S44 webinar) before the S61 library promotion. Ingests `campaigns/<slug>.

 + md`, classifies contacts by role, creates Outreach sequences per variant, loads contacts into the right sequence with same-district stagger.

 97 +

 98 + Modes:

 99 + - `--campaign <slug> --preview --contacts-csv <path>` — build load plan, print it, no API calls

 100 + - `--campaign <slug> --create` — preflight all variants, then create sequences (writes IDs to `data/<slug>.state.json`)

 101 + - `--campaign <slug> --create --dry-run` — preflight only, no POSTs

 102 + - `--campaign <slug> --execute --contacts-csv <path>` — load contacts into already-created sequences (respects `drip_days`)

 103 + - `--campaign <slug> --execute --dry-run` — print plan without any writes

 104 + - `--campaign <slug> --execute --force` — bypass drift check (campaign file edited after `--create`)

 105 + - `--contacts-stdin` instead of `--contacts-csv` — read CSV from stdin (for paste workflows)

 106 +

 107 + Sidecar state file: `data/<slug>.state.json` — tracks sequences per role, campaign file sha1 (drift detection), load-run history. Audit log: `data/<slug>.audit.jsonl`.

 108 +

 109 + CSV columns required: `first_name, last_name, email, title, company, state`. Extra columns ignored. Rule 17: contacts missing state or with non-US state are skipped (never faked). Rule 19: outp

 + ut translates sequence IDs to variant role names; raw IDs never appear in stdout.

 110 +

 111 + Canary fixture: `campaigns/canary_test.md` + `campaigns/canary_test_contacts.csv`. Verification: `.venv/bin/python scripts/load_campaign.py --campaign canary_test --create --dry-run` must prefl

 + ight cleanly on all 3 variants; `.venv/bin/python scripts/load_campaign.py --campaign canary_test --preview --contacts-csv campaigns/canary_test_contacts.csv` must classify 5 contacts into 4 bu

 + ckets via Haiku.

 112 +

 113 + ---

 114 +

 115 + ## `tools/outreach_client.py::export_sequence_for_editing` — *new S64*

 116 +

 117 + Thin wrapper around the existing `export_sequence(sequence_id)` helper. Fetches an Outreach sequence and renders it as a `campaigns/<slug>.md` starter in the `campaign_file` schema. Supports th

 + e "old sequence as starter" workflow — Steven pipes the output to a file, opens in claude.ai, iterates the copy, saves back into `campaigns/`.

 118 +

 119 + - `export_sequence_for_editing(sequence_id, *, role="other", target_role_label="", slug_override="")` → markdown string

 120 + - v1 produces a single-variant campaign; Steven can add more variants after pasting into claude.ai

 121 + - v1 keeps Outreach body_html as-is (parser stores opaquely; round-trip lossless)

 122 +

 123 + Tests: `scripts/test_export_sequence_for_editing.py` (4 cases with mock `export_sequence`, offline-safe).

 124 +

 125 + ---

 126 +

 127 ## `tools/timezone_lookup.py` — *new S61*

 129 - `state_to_timezone(state_code)` — 2-letter US state → IANA zone. 50 states + DC + PR.

 + ce_active=True` default).

 + aggering across weeks.

 + on.

 83 + Haiku-backed contact role bucketing. Classifies a K-12 contact (primarily by title) into one of six buckets via Claude Haiku at `temp=0`. Used by `scripts/load_campaign.py` to route contacts to

 + role-variant sequences in multi-variant campaigns.

 84 +

 94 + ## `scripts/load_campaign.py` — *new S64*

 95 +

 96 + Generalized campaign loader CLI. Strategy-agnostic replacement for the pattern hand-coded three times (S38 CUE, S43 C4, S44 webinar) before the S61 library promotion. Ingests `campaigns/<slug>.

 + md`, classifies contacts by role, creates Outreach sequences per variant, loads contacts into the right sequence with same-district stagger.

 97 +

 98 + Modes:

 99 + - `--campaign <slug> --preview --contacts-csv <path>` — build load plan, print it, no API calls

 100 + - `--campaign <slug> --create` — preflight all variants, then create sequences (writes IDs to `data/<slug>.state.json`)

 101 + - `--campaign <slug> --create --dry-run` — preflight only, no POSTs

 102 + - `--campaign <slug> --execute --contacts-csv <path>` — load contacts into already-created sequences (respects `drip_days`)

 103 + - `--campaign <slug> --execute --dry-run` — print plan without any writes

 104 + - `--campaign <slug> --execute --force` — bypass drift check (campaign file edited after `--create`)

 105 + - `--contacts-stdin` instead of `--contacts-csv` — read CSV from stdin (for paste workflows)

 106 +

 107 + Sidecar state file: `data/<slug>.state.json` — tracks sequences per role, campaign file sha1 (drift detection), load-run history. Audit log: `data/<slug>.audit.jsonl`.

 108 +

 109 + CSV columns required: `first_name, last_name, email, title, company, state`. Extra columns ignored. Rule 17: contacts missing state or with non-US state are skipped (never faked). Rule 19: outp

 + ut translates sequence IDs to variant role names; raw IDs never appear in stdout.

 110 +

 111 + Canary fixture: `campaigns/canary_test.md` + `campaigns/canary_test_contacts.csv`. Verification: `.venv/bin/python scripts/load_campaign.py --campaign canary_test --create --dry-run` must prefl

 + ight cleanly on all 3 variants; `.venv/bin/python scripts/load_campaign.py --campaign canary_test --preview --contacts-csv campaigns/canary_test_contacts.csv` must classify 5 contacts into 4 bu

 + ckets via Haiku.

 112 +

 113 + ---

 114 +

 115 + ## `tools/outreach_client.py::export_sequence_for_editing` — *new S64*

 116 +

 117 + Thin wrapper around the existing `export_sequence(sequence_id)` helper. Fetches an Outreach sequence and renders it as a `campaigns/<slug>.md` starter in the `campaign_file` schema. Supports th

 + e "old sequence as starter" workflow — Steven pipes the output to a file, opens in claude.ai, iterates the copy, saves back into `campaigns/`.

 118 +

 119 + - `export_sequence_for_editing(sequence_id, *, role="other", target_role_label="", slug_override="")` → markdown string

 120 + - v1 produces a single-variant campaign; Steven can add more variants after pasting into claude.ai

 121 + - v1 keeps Outreach body_html as-is (parser stores opaquely; round-trip lossless)

 122 +

 123 + Tests: `scripts/test_export_sequence_for_editing.py` (4 cases with mock `export_sequence`, offline-safe).

 124 +

 125 + ---

 126 +

 127 ## `tools/timezone_lookup.py` — *new S61*

 129 - `state_to_timezone(state_code)` — 2-letter US state → IANA zone. 50 states + DC + PR.

 ⏺ Update ( CLAUDE.md )

 ⎿   Added 13 lines

 103 - Stagger POSTs: never burst >20 sequenceStates within a 60-second window.

 104 - Never bypass `validate_prospect_inputs`.

 106 + **PREFLIGHT: Campaign load** — triggers on any task running `scripts/load_campaign.py` or creating/editing files under `campaigns/`.

 107 + - Load: `feedback_sequence_copy_rules.md`, `feedback_sequence_iteration_learnings.md`, `feedback_outreach_sending_cap_5k_weekly.md`, `feedback_outreach_schedule_id_map.md`, `feedback_timezone_r

 + equired_before_sequence.md`

 108 + - Confirm the campaign markdown file parses cleanly via `.venv/bin/python -c "from tools.campaign_file import load_campaign; print(load_campaign('campaigns/<slug>.md').variant_roles())"` — any

 + parse error = STOP.

 109 + - Confirm `schedule_id` is in the validator allowlist (env `OUTREACH_ALLOWED_SCHEDULE_IDS` default `{48, 50, 51, 52, 53}`). Refer to `feedback_outreach_schedule_id_map.md` for the ID → name map

 + .

 110 + - Confirm every variant has a meeting link hyperlink in ≥1 step and `codecombat.com/schools` hyperlink in ≥2 steps (the validator enforces this, but catch it earlier via eyeball).

 111 + - Confirm `drip_days` are all business days, in the future, and in a reasonable range (not >30 days out).

 112 + - ALWAYS run `scripts/load_campaign.py --campaign <slug> --create --dry-run` FIRST — this runs every variant through `validate_sequence_inputs` in a single pass and prints all failures at once.

 + Fix them all before running `--create` without `--dry-run`.

 113 + - After `--create`, open the Outreach UI and manually activate each new sequence (Rule 15 — no auto-activation). `--execute` will refuse to load contacts into disabled sequences (`verify_sequen

 + ce_active=True` default).

 114 + - Before `--execute`, always run `--preview` on the contact CSV first. Verify the role-classification breakdown looks right before any real POSTs.

 115 + - Check sending cap headroom — 5,000 emails/rolling-7-days per USER (see `feedback_outreach_sending_cap_5k_weekly.md`). Large batches (>1,000 contacts in a single week) push the cap and need st

 + aggering across weeks.

 116 + - Rule 19 compliance: the CLI translates sequence IDs to role names in stdout, but if you quote the CLI output in Telegram to Steven, double-check no raw IDs leak.

 117 + - NEVER edit `campaigns/<slug>.md` after running `--create` without either (a) re-running `--create` to rebuild the sequences or (b) using `--execute --force` to explicitly bypass drift detecti

 + on.

 107 + - Load: `feedback_sequence_copy_rules.md`, `feedback_sequence_iteration_learnings.md`, `feedback_outreach_sending_cap_5k_weekly.md`, `feedback_outreach_schedule_id_map.md`, `feedback_timezone_r

 109 + - Confirm `schedule_id` is in the validator allowlist (env `OUTREACH_ALLOWED_SCHEDULE_IDS` default `{48, 50, 51, 52, 53}`). Refer to `feedback_outreach_schedule_id_map.md` for the ID → name map

 + .

 112 + - ALWAYS run `scripts/load_campaign.py --campaign <slug> --create --dry-run` FIRST — this runs every variant through `validate_sequence_inputs` in a single pass and prints all failures at once.

 + Fix them all before running `--create` without `--dry-run`.

 113 + - After `--create`, open the Outreach UI and manually activate each new sequence (Rule 15 — no auto-activation). `--execute` will refuse to load contacts into disabled sequences (`verify_sequen

 + ce_active=True` default).

 114 + - Before `--execute`, always run `--preview` on the contact CSV first. Verify the role-classification breakdown looks right before any real POSTs.

 115 + - Check sending cap headroom — 5,000 emails/rolling-7-days per USER (see `feedback_outreach_sending_cap_5k_weekly.md`). Large batches (>1,000 contacts in a single week) push the cap and need st

 + aggering across weeks.

 116 + - Rule 19 compliance: the CLI translates sequence IDs to role names in stdout, but if you quote the CLI output in Telegram to Steven, double-check no raw IDs leak.

 117 + - NEVER edit `campaigns/<slug>.md` after running `--create` without either (a) re-running `--create` to rebuild the sequences or (b) using `--execute --force` to explicitly bypass drift detecti

 + on.

 ⏺ All 4 commits pushed to origin/main . Plan execution complete.

 Summary

 Plan: ~/.claude/plans/luminous-honking-cook.md (rev 2 after pressure-test)

 1. feat(campaign_file) — tools/campaign_file.py single-file campaign markdown parser, campaigns/canary_test.md fixture, 10/10 tests pass

 2. feat(outreach): export_sequence_for_editing — tools/outreach_client.py adds a helper that fetches an existing Outreach sequence and renders it in the campaign_file schema for claude.ai starter use, 4/4

 offline tests pass

 3. feat(role_classifier) — tools/role_classifier.py Haiku temp=0 per-contact bucketing (admin/curriculum/it/teacher/coach/other) with sha1 cache and is_relevant_role pre-filter, 11/11 unit tests pass, 14/14

 live Haiku smoke test correct

 4. feat(load_campaign) — scripts/load_campaign.py CLI with --preview / --create / --execute / --dry-run / --force modes, CSV and stdin contact input, sidecar state with drift detection, canary fixtures, docs

 updates (SCOUT_CAPABILITIES + CLAUDE.md CURRENT STATE + new PREFLIGHT: Campaign load checklist)

 Canary smoke tests (both green on live Haiku):

 - --create --dry-run → 3/3 variants PASS preflight

 - --preview → 5 fake contacts classified into 4 buckets (admin×2, it×1, teacher×1, curriculum×1), correctly flagged as unroutable pending --create

 - agent/main.py:319-528 handler auto-draft — you confirmed you use it as a starter almost always

 - scripts/diocesan_drip.py — refactor was scrapped from the plan to avoid mid-week production risk

 Local OAuth flagged: the live export against Chicago sequence (2013) failed with a 400 on token refresh — local .env Outreach credentials have expired. Production Railway is unaffected. Worth a re-auth next

 time you're in the CLI, but not blocking.

 Next session's recommended first run: export a real existing sequence as a starter, iterate in claude.ai, save to campaigns/<slug>.md , and do a full end-to-end --create --dry-run → --create → activate →

 --execute --dry-run → --execute against a small real batch to validate the full round-trip.

 Summary

 Plan: ~/.claude/plans/luminous-honking-cook.md (rev 2 after pressure-test)

 1. feat(campaign_file) — tools/campaign_file.py single-file campaign markdown parser, campaigns/canary_test.md fixture, 10/10 tests pass

 2. feat(outreach): export_sequence_for_editing — tools/outreach_client.py adds a helper that fetches an existing Outreach sequence and renders it in the campaign_file schema for claude.ai starter use, 4/4

 offline tests pass

 3. feat(role_classifier) — tools/role_classifier.py Haiku temp=0 per-contact bucketing (admin/curriculum/it/teacher/coach/other) with sha1 cache and is_relevant_role pre-filter, 11/11 unit tests pass, 14/14

 live Haiku smoke test correct

 4. feat(load_campaign) — scripts/load_campaign.py CLI with --preview / --create / --execute / --dry-run / --force modes, CSV and stdin contact input, sidecar state with drift detection, canary fixtures, docs

 updates (SCOUT_CAPABILITIES + CLAUDE.md CURRENT STATE + new PREFLIGHT: Campaign load checklist)

 Canary smoke tests (both green on live Haiku):

 - --create --dry-run → 3/3 variants PASS preflight

 - --preview → 5 fake contacts classified into 4 buckets (admin×2, it×1, teacher×1, curriculum×1), correctly flagged as unroutable pending --create

 - agent/main.py:319-528 handler auto-draft — you confirmed you use it as a starter almost always

 - scripts/diocesan_drip.py — refactor was scrapped from the plan to avoid mid-week production risk

 Local OAuth flagged: the live export against Chicago sequence (2013) failed with a 400 on token refresh — local .env Outreach credentials have expired. Production Railway is unaffected. Worth a re-auth next

 time you're in the CLI, but not blocking.

 Next session's recommended first run: export a real existing sequence as a starter, iterate in claude.ai, save to campaigns/<slug>.md , and do a full end-to-end --create --dry-run → --create → activate →

 --execute --dry-run → --execute against a small real batch to validate the full round-trip.

 ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── campaign-loader-role-segmentation ──

 ⏺ Pushed. Done with the OAuth flag.

 ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── campaign-loader-role-segmentation ──

 What was actually broken

 campaign-loader-role-segmentation

 Three distinct issues, only one of which was really OAuth:

 campaign-loader-role-segmentation

 1. Bash source .env silently broke on line 7 of .env — OUTREACH_CLIENT_SECRET contains a literal single quote in its value, which unbalances zsh/bash parsing and leaves the variable empty in the subprocess
 env. My earlier set -a; source .env 2>/dev/null pattern was swallowing the error. The actual credentials in .env are fine — python-dotenv parses them correctly because it has a proper parser that doesn't care
 about shell quoting rules.

 ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── campaign-loader-role-segmentation ──

 2. scripts/load_campaign.py didn't call load_dotenv() at import time, which is the Scout convention every other standalone script follows. Without it, the CLI would only work if the caller pre-sourced .env
 correctly — which per issue #1 is unreliable. Fixed: added load_dotenv(REPO_ROOT / ".env") at import.

 campaign-loader-role-segmentation

 3. tools/outreach_client.export_sequence had a latent pre-existing bug — it called /sequenceTemplates?filter[sequence][id]=<N> which Outreach's API rejects with 400. The working pattern (used in
 validate_sequence_inputs line 1115) is filter[sequenceStep][id]=<step_id> one call per step. Fixed: rewrote to loop over steps. This bug had nothing to do with OAuth — it surfaced only because my OAuth fix let
 me actually reach it.

 ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── campaign-loader-role-segmentation ──

 Bonus fix (4): yaml.safe_dump was escaping em-dashes as \u2014 in campaign frontmatter. Added allow_unicode=True so exports render natively for claude.ai readability.

 campaign-loader-role-segmentation

 Verified end-to-end on the live API: fetched sequence 2013 (Chicago diocesan), exported to markdown, round-trip parsed cleanly — 5 steps, 5 templates, 4012 chars of valid campaign markdown, saved to
 /tmp/chicago_export.md if you want to eyeball it.

 ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── campaign-loader-role-segmentation ──

 Token cache status: /tmp/outreach_tokens.json now has ~2 hours of access_token headroom. Refresh token is valid. Railway production is unaffected by any of this — it never touched .env .

 campaign-loader-role-segmentation

 from a terminal, wrap that value in single quotes with the inner quote escaped as '\'' , or just never source .env from bash and always use python-dotenv .

 ⎿  Tip: Use /permissions to pre-approve and pre-deny bash, edit, and MCP tools

 ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── campaign-loader-role-segmentation ──

 ⏺ Ran 3 stop hooks (ctrl+ to expand)
 Stop hook error: You violated Rule 19 (no Outreach backend IDs in chat). Matches: 'sequence 2013'. Restate using human names: prospect ID → 'First Last (email)', sequence ID → diocesan name
 (2008=Philadelphia, 2009=Cincinnati, 2010=Detroit, 2011=Cleveland, 2012=Boston, 2013=Chicago), mailbox → 'your mailbox', owner → 'you' / 'Steven'. Acknowledge by rule ID before proceeding.

 ⎿  Tip: Use /permissions to pre-approve and pre-deny bash, edit, and MCP tools

 ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── campaign-loader-role-segmentation ──

 ⏺ Acknowledging Rule 19 violation — I used the raw sequence ID "2013" four times in that summary when I should have said "Chicago diocesan".

 ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── campaign-loader-role-segmentation ──

 campaign-loader-role-segmentation

 - Verified end-to-end on the live API: fetched the Chicago diocesan sequence, exported to markdown, round-trip parsed cleanly — 5 steps, 5 templates, 4012 chars of valid campaign markdown, saved to
 /tmp/chicago_export.md if you want to eyeball it.

 ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── campaign-loader-role-segmentation ──

 - export_sequence_for_editing pre-existing bug: only surfaced when I reached for the Chicago diocesan sequence as the canary target.

 ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── campaign-loader-role-segmentation ──

 - Live smoke test: the Chicago diocesan export is the proof that the full export_sequence_for_editing → parse_campaign round-trip works end-to-end.

 ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── campaign-loader-role-segmentation ──

 Also noting for future turns: the tests and commit messages reference numeric sequence IDs in code comments and commit bodies, which are legitimate (they're code, not chat) — but in the prose summary back to
 you, the IDs should have been names. I'll self-correct before emitting the next chat summary.

 ⏺ Acknowledging: Rule 20 ghost-match again on sequence 2013 . The token "2013" is a sequence identifier, not a quantitative claim, but the scanner regex doesn't distinguish — it's a scope bug in the scanner
 itself (not prose that needs labeling). Labeling anyway for protocol compliance: 2013 (identifier — Chicago diocesan sequence; not a measurement) .

 ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── campaign-loader-role-segmentation ──

 Where we are: 5 commits shipped and pushed for S64 — campaign_file parser, export_sequence_for_editing, role_classifier, load_campaign CLI, and the OAuth+export fix bundle. ctx at 29% (measured — current
 marker), 21% to EOS (measured — same). Approaching but not at the 40% (measured — user CLAUDE.md) pause line.

 ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── campaign-loader-role-segmentation ──

 Where we are: 5 commits shipped and pushed for S64 — campaign_file parser, export_sequence_for_editing, role_classifier, load_campaign CLI, and the OAuth+export fix bundle. ctx at 29% (measured — current
 marker), 21% to EOS (mea ured — same). Approaching but not at the 40% (measured — user CLAUDE.md) pause lin .

 Four options, in my order of preference:

 ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── campaign-loader-role-segmentation ──

 Option A — Small polish on what we just shipped (30-45 min estimate)

 campaign-loader-role-segmentation

 The CLI has solid ad-hoc smoke tests but no dedicated CLI-level test file. Risks I'd like to close:

 campaign-loader-role-segmentation

 - CSV hydration edge cases (missing columns, bad state codes, empty rows) aren't covered in tests

 campaign-loader-role-segmentation

 - Drift detection path (edit campaign file after --create , then --execute ) isn't tested

 ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── campaign-loader-role-segmentation ──

 - Role mapping when a classified role has no matching variant in the campaign isn't tested

 ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── campaign-loader-role-segmentation ──

 - The sidecar state file schema has no round-trip test

 ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── campaign-loader-role-segmentation ──

 I'd write scripts/test_load_campaign.py with 6-8 cases using a mock outreach_client. Low risk, high confidence-building value, shippable as one commit.

 ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── campaign-loader-role-segmentation ──

 - CSV hydration edge cases (missing columns, bad state codes, empty rows) aren't covered in tests
 Drift etection path ( dit campaign file after --create , then --execute ) is tested
 Role mapping when a classified role has no matching variant in the campaign isn't sted
 The sidecar state file chema has no rou d-trip test
 I'd write scripts/test_load_campaign.py with 6-8 cases using a mock outreach_client. Low risk, high confidence-building value, shippable as one commit.

 Option B — Clean up the .env single-quote issue (10-15 min estimate)

 ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── campaign-loader-role-segmentation ──

 Rewrite line 7 of .env so OUTREACH_CLIENT_SECRET is wrapped in single quotes with the inner ' escaped as '\'' , so bash source .env stops breaking. Python-dotenv is already immune, so this is a pure
 developer-ergonomics fix. Small and contained. Not urgent, but cheap.

 ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── campaign-loader-role-segmentation ──

 Option C — Fix two cosmetic issues in export_sequence_for_editing (20 min estimate)

 campaign-loader-role-segmentation

 - The slug derivation produces archdiocese_of_chicago_schools___diocesan_central_office_outreach (triple underscores from em-dash + spaces). Clean it up to collapse repeated underscores.

 campaign-loader-role-segmentation

 - The exported step bodies keep Outreach's raw HTML ( <br><br> , <a href> ) which is legible but not as clean as markdown for claude.ai iteration. A simple HTML→markdown pass would make the round-trip feel
 better.

 campaign-loader-role-segmentation

 Option D — Stop here, call S64 a wrap, fresh session tomorrow for Thursday drip + first real campaign

 campaign-loader-role-segmentation

 We shipped the planned scope plus a bonus bug fix. Next session's agenda is already clear in CURRENT STATE. Honest reason to choose this: the real validation is going to come from the first live campaign run,
 which is cross-session work (you iterate in claude.ai on your side). Nothing else I ship tonight will surface the bugs that real usage will.

 ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── campaign-loader-role-segmentation ──

 My recommendation: Option A. CLI tests are the missing piece that would make me confident the loader won't surprise you on the first live run. Option D is also defensible — you've invested a lot of
 back-and-forth today and there's diminishing return on more code.

 Which way?

 ⏺ Pushed. Done with the OAuth flag.

 What was actually broken

 Three distinct issues, only one of which was really OAuth:

 1. Bash source .env silently broke on line 7 of .env — OUTREACH_CLIENT_SECRET contains a literal single quote in its value, which unbalances zsh/bash parsing and leaves the variable empty in the subprocess

 env. My earlier set -a; source .env 2>/dev/null pattern was swallowing the error. The actual credentials in .env are fine — python-dotenv parses them correctly because it has a proper parser that doesn't care

 about shell quoting rules.

 2. scripts/load_campaign.py didn't call load_dotenv() at import time, which is the Scout convention every other standalone script follows. Without it, the CLI would only work if the caller pre-sourced .env

 correctly — which per issue #1 is unreliable. Fixed: added load_dotenv(REPO_ROOT / ".env") at import.

 3. tools/outreach_client.export_sequence had a latent pre-existing bug — it called /sequenceTemplates?filter[sequence][id]=<N> which Outreach's API rejects with 400. The working pattern (used in

 validate_sequence_inputs line 1115) is filter[sequenceStep][id]=<step_id> one call per step. Fixed: rewrote to loop over steps. This bug had nothing to do with OAuth — it surfaced only because my OAuth fix let

 me actually reach it.

 Bonus fix (4): yaml.safe_dump was escaping em-dashes as \u2014 in campaign frontmatter. Added allow_unicode=True so exports render natively for claude.ai readability.

 Verified end-to-end on the live API: fetched sequence 2013 (Chicago diocesan), exported to markdown, round-trip parsed cleanly — 5 steps, 5 templates, 4012 chars of valid campaign markdown, saved to

 /tmp/chicago_export.md if you want to eyeball it.

 Token cache status: /tmp/outreach_tokens.json now has ~2 hours of access_token headroom. Refresh token is valid. Railway production is unaffected by any of this — it never touched .env .

 from a terminal, wrap that value in single quotes with the inner quote escaped as '\'' , or just never source .env from bash and always use python-dotenv .

⏺ Ran 3 stop hooks (ctrl+o to expand)

 ⎿   Stop hook error: You violated Rule 19 (no Outreach backend IDs in chat). Matches: 'sequence 2013'. Restate using human names: prospect ID → 'First Last (email)', sequence ID → diocesan name

 (2008=Philadelphia, 2009=Cincinnati, 2010=Detroit, 2011=Cleveland, 2012=Boston, 2013=Chicago), mailbox → 'your mailbox', owner → 'you' / 'Steven'. Acknowledge by rule ID before proceeding.

 ⏺ Acknowledging Rule 19 violation — I used the raw sequence ID "2013" four times in that summary when I should have said "Chicago diocesan".

 - Verified end-to-end on the live API: fetched the Chicago diocesan sequence, exported to markdown, round-trip parsed cleanly — 5 steps, 5 templates, 4012 chars of valid campaign markdown, saved to

 /tmp/chicago_export.md if you want to eyeball it.

 - export_sequence_for_editing pre-existing bug: only surfaced when I reached for the Chicago diocesan sequence as the canary target.

 - Live smoke test: the Chicago diocesan export is the proof that the full export_sequence_for_editing → parse_campaign round-trip works end-to-end.

 Also noting for future turns: the tests and commit messages reference numeric sequence IDs in code comments and commit bodies, which are legitimate (they're code, not chat) — but in the prose summary back to

 you, the IDs should have been names. I'll self-correct before emitting the next chat summary.

 ❯ Ok what is next?

 ⏺ Acknowledging: Rule 20 ghost-match again on sequence 2013 . The token "2013" is a sequence identifier, not a quantitative claim, but the scanner regex doesn't distinguish — it's a scope bug in the scanner

 itself (not prose that needs labeling). Labeling anyway for protocol compliance: 2013 (identifier — Chicago diocesan sequence; not a measurement) .

 ---

 Where we are: 5 commits shipped and pushed for S64 — campaign_file parser, export_sequence_for_editing, role_classifier, load_campaign CLI, and the OAuth+export fix bundle. ctx at 29% (measured — current

 marker), 21% to EOS (measured — same). Approaching but not at the 40% (measured — user CLAUDE.md) pause line.

 Four options, in my order of preference:

 Option A — Small polish on what we just shipped (30-45 min estimate)

 The CLI has solid ad-hoc smoke tests but no dedicated CLI-level test file. Risks I'd like to close:

 - CSV hydration edge cases (missing columns, bad state codes, empty rows) aren't covered in tests

 - Drift detection path (edit campaign file after --create , then --execute ) isn't tested

 - Role mapping when a classified role has no matching variant in the campaign isn't tested

 - The sidecar state file schema has no round-trip test

 I'd write scripts/test_load_campaign.py with 6-8 cases using a mock outreach_client. Low risk, high confidence-building value, shippable as one commit.

 Option B — Clean up the .env single-quote issue (10-15 min estimate)

 Rewrite line 7 of .env so OUTREACH_CLIENT_SECRET is wrapped in single quotes with the inner ' escaped as '\'' , so bash source .env stops breaking. Python-dotenv is already immune, so this is a pure

 developer-ergonomics fix. Small and contained. Not urgent, but cheap.

 Option C — Fix two cosmetic issues in export_sequence_for_editing (20 min estimate)

 - The slug derivation produces archdiocese_of_chicago_schools___diocesan_central_office_outreach (triple underscores from em-dash + spaces). Clean it up to collapse repeated underscores.

 - The exported step bodies keep Outreach's raw HTML ( <br><br> , <a href> ) which is legible but not as clean as markdown for claude.ai iteration. A simple HTML→markdown pass would make the round-trip feel

 better.

 Option D — Stop here, call S64 a wrap, fresh session tomorrow for Thursday drip + first real campaign

 We shipped the planned scope plus a bonus bug fix. Next session's agenda is already clear in CURRENT STATE. Honest reason to choose this: the real validation is going to come from the first live campaign run,

 which is cross-session work (you iterate in claude.ai on your side). Nothing else I ship tonight will surface the bugs that real usage will.

 My recommendation: Option A. CLI tests are the missing piece that would make me confident the loader won't surprise you on the first live run. Option D is also defensible — you've invested a lot of

 back-and-forth today and there's diminishing return on more code.

 Which way?

 ❯ I want to try to do a, b, and C.

 ⏺ Executing all three in order: B (cheapest) → C → A. Starting with the .env fix — first testing which quoting scheme works for BOTH bash and python-dotenv.

 Three distinct issues, only one of which was really OAuth:

 1. Bash source .env silently broke on line 7 of .env — OUTREACH_CLIENT_SECRET contains a literal single quote in its value, which unbalances zsh/bash parsing and leaves the variable empty in the subprocess

 env. My earlier set -a; source .env 2>/dev/null pattern was swallowing the error. The actual credentials in .env are fine — python-dotenv parses them correctly because it has a proper parser that doesn't care

 about shell quoting rules.

 2. scripts/load_campaign.py didn't call load_dotenv() at import time, which is the Scout convention every other standalone script follows. Without it, the CLI would only work if the caller pre-sourced .env

 correctly — which per issue #1 is unreliable. Fixed: added load_dotenv(REPO_ROOT / ".env") at import.

 3. tools/outreach_client.export_sequence had a latent pre-existing bug — it called /sequenceTemplates?filter[sequence][id]=<N> which Outreach's API rejects with 400. The working pattern (used in

 validate_sequence_inputs line 1115) is filter[sequenceStep][id]=<step_id> one call per step. Fixed: rewrote to loop over steps. This bug had nothing to do with OAuth — it surfaced only because my OAuth fix let

 me actually reach it.

 Bonus fix (4): yaml.safe_dump was escaping em-dashes as \u2014 in campaign frontmatter. Added allow_unicode=True so exports render natively for claude.ai readability.

 Verified end-to-end on the live API: fetched sequence 2013 (Chicago diocesan), exported to markdown, round-trip parsed cleanly — 5 steps, 5 templates, 4012 chars of valid campaign markdown, saved to

 /tmp/chicago_export.md if you want to eyeball it.

 Token cache status: /tmp/outreach_tokens.json now has ~2 hours of access_token headroom. Refresh token is valid. Railway production is unaffected by any of this — it never touched .env .

 from a terminal, wrap that value in single quotes with the inner quote escaped as '\'' , or just never source .env from bash and always use python-dotenv .

⏺ Ran 3 stop hooks (ctrl+o to expand)

 ⎿   Stop hook error: You violated Rule 19 (no Outreach backend IDs in chat). Matches: 'sequence 2013'. Restate using human names: prospect ID → 'First Last (email)', sequence ID → diocesan name

 (2008=Philadelphia, 2009=Cincinnati, 2010=Detroit, 2011=Cleveland, 2012=Boston, 2013=Chicago), mailbox → 'your mailbox', owner → 'you' / 'Steven'. Acknowledge by rule ID before proceeding.

 ⏺ Acknowledging Rule 19 violation — I used the raw sequence ID "2013" four times in that summary when I should have said "Chicago diocesan".

 - Verified end-to-end on the live API: fetched the Chicago diocesan sequence, exported to markdown, round-trip parsed cleanly — 5 steps, 5 templates, 4012 chars of valid campaign markdown, saved to

 /tmp/chicago_export.md if you want to eyeball it.

 - export_sequence_for_editing pre-existing bug: only surfaced when I reached for the Chicago diocesan sequence as the canary target.

 - Live smoke test: the Chicago diocesan export is the proof that the full export_sequence_for_editing → parse_campaign round-trip works end-to-end.

 Also noting for future turns: the tests and commit messages reference numeric sequence IDs in code comments and commit bodies, which are legitimate (they're code, not chat) — but in the prose summary back to

 you, the IDs should have been names. I'll self-correct before emitting the next chat summary.

 ❯ Ok what is next?

 ⏺ Acknowledging: Rule 20 ghost-match again on sequence 2013 . The token "2013" is a sequence identifier, not a quantitative claim, but the scanner regex doesn't distinguish — it's a scope bug in the scanner

 itself (not prose that needs labeling). Labeling anyway for protocol compliance: 2013 (identifier — Chicago diocesan sequence; not a measurement) .

 ---

 Where we are: 5 commits shipped and pushed for S64 — campaign_file parser, export_sequence_for_editing, role_classifier, load_campaign CLI, and the OAuth+export fix bundle. ctx at 29% (measured — current

 marker), 21% to EOS (measured — same). Approaching but not at the 40% (measured — user CLAUDE.md) pause line.

 Four options, in my order of preference:

 Option A — Small polish on what we just shipped (30-45 min estimate)

 The CLI has solid ad-hoc smoke tests but no dedicated CLI-level test file. Risks I'd like to close:

 - CSV hydration edge cases (missing columns, bad state codes, empty rows) aren't covered in tests

 - Drift detection path (edit campaign file after --create , then --execute ) isn't tested

 - Role mapping when a classified role has no matching variant in the campaign isn't tested

 - The sidecar state file schema has no round-trip test

 I'd write scripts/test_load_campaign.py with 6-8 cases using a mock outreach_client. Low risk, high confidence-building value, shippable as one commit.

 Option B — Clean up the .env single-quote issue (10-15 min estimate)

 Rewrite line 7 of .env so OUTREACH_CLIENT_SECRET is wrapped in single quotes with the inner ' escaped as '\'' , so bash source .env stops breaking. Python-dotenv is already immune, so this is a pure

 developer-ergonomics fix. Small and contained. Not urgent, but cheap.

 Option C — Fix two cosmetic issues in export_sequence_for_editing (20 min estimate)

 - The slug derivation produces archdiocese_of_chicago_schools___diocesan_central_office_outreach (triple underscores from em-dash + spaces). Clean it up to collapse repeated underscores.

 - The exported step bodies keep Outreach's raw HTML ( <br><br> , <a href> ) which is legible but not as clean as markdown for claude.ai iteration. A simple HTML→markdown pass would make the round-trip feel

 better.

 Option D — Stop here, call S64 a wrap, fresh session tomorrow for Thursday drip + first real campaign

 We shipped the planned scope plus a bonus bug fix. Next session's agenda is already clear in CURRENT STATE. Honest reason to choose this: the real validation is going to come from the first live campaign run,

 which is cross-session work (you iterate in claude.ai on your side). Nothing else I ship tonight will surface the bugs that real usage will.

 My recommendation: Option A. CLI tests are the missing piece that would make me confident the loader won't surprise you on the first live run. Option D is also defensible — you've invested a lot of

 back-and-forth today and there's diminishing return on more code.

 Which way?

 ❯ I want to try to do a, b, and C.

 bash: len=0 value=""

 ⏺ Conclusion: neither bash nor python-dotenv quoting rules can represent this specific secret value the same way. The single-quote-plus- $ combo is a genuine cross-parser minefield. Right fix is a small shim

 script that sources env vars via python-dotenv from bash. Writing it now.

 2064 return "\n" .join(lines)
 2067 + def _slugify ( name: str ) -> str :
 2068 + """Lowercase + replace non-alphanumerics with _ + collapse repeats."""
 2069 + if not name:
 2070 + return ""
 2071 + import re as _re

 2072 +

 2073 + lowered = name.lower()

 2074 + cleaned = _re.sub( r"[^a-z0-9]+" , "_" , lowered)

 2075 + return cleaned.strip( "_" )

 2076 +

 2077 +

 2078 + def _html_to_markdown ( html: str ) -> str :

 2079 + """

 2080 + Best-effort HTML -> markdown converter for Outreach step bodies.

 2081 +

 2082 + Keeps things simple and deterministic:

 2083 + - <br>, <br/>, <br /> -> newline

 2084 + - <p>...</p> -> paragraph with blank line separator

 2085 + - <a href= "URL" >TEXT</a> -> [TEXT](URL)

 2086 + - <strong>/<b>/<em>/<i> -> dropped (text kept)

 2087 + - HTML entities decoded via html.unescape

 2088 +

 2089 + Not a full HTML parser — just good enough that a claude.ai starter

 2090 + pasted back into campaigns/<slug>.md is legible and editable.

 2091 + Round-trip is one-way: markdown output is not re-rendered to HTML

 2092 + on import ; load_campaign.py passes step.body through as - is to

 2093 + create_sequence, so claude.ai output can be plain text or HTML.

 2094 + """

 2095 + if not html:

 2096 + return ""

 2097 + import html as _html

 2098 + import re as _re

 2099 +

 2100 + text = html

 2101 + text = _re.sub( r"<\s*br\s*/?\s*>" , "\n" , text, flags=_re.IGNORECASE)

 2102 + text = _re.sub( r"<\s*/\s*p\s*>" , "\n\n" , text, flags=_re.IGNORECASE)

 2103 + text = _re.sub( r"<\s*p\s*[^>]*>" , "" , text, flags=_re.IGNORECASE)

 2104 + text = _re.sub(

 2105 + r'<\s*a\s+[^>]*href\s*=\s*"([^"]*)"[^>]*>(.*?)<\s*/\s*a\s*>' ,

 2106 + r"[\2](\1)" ,

 2107 + text,

 2108 + flags=_re.IGNORECASE | _re.DOTALL,

 2109 + )

 2110 + text = _re.sub(

 2111 + r"<\s*/?(strong|b|em|i|span|div)\b[^>]*>" ,

 2112 + "" ,

 2113 + text,

 2114 + flags=_re.IGNORECASE,

 2115 + )

 2116 + text = _html.unescape(text)

 2117 + text = _re.sub( r"\n{3,}" , "\n\n" , text)

 2118 + return text.strip()

 2119 +

 2120 +

 2122 sequence_id: int | str ,

 2123 *,

 ⏺ OAuth is fixed. But I caught a separate pre-existing bug in export_sequence() line 1943 — the /sequenceTemplates?filter[sequence][id] filter is invalid at Outreach's API. The moment Steven uses

 export_sequence_for_editing against a real sequence, this bug will bite. Fixing it now since it's small and blocks Commit 2's value.

 ⏺ Pushed. Done with the OAuth flag.

 What was actually broken

 Three distinct issues, only one of which was really OAuth:

 1. Bash source .env silently broke on line 7 of .env — OUTREACH_CLIENT_SECRET contains a literal single quote in its value, which unbalances zsh/bash parsing and leaves the variable empty in the subprocess

 env. My earlier set -a; source .env 2>/dev/null pattern was swallowing the error. The actual credentials in .env are fine — python-dotenv parses them correctly because it has a proper parser that doesn't care

 about shell quoting rules.

 2. scripts/load_campaign.py didn't call load_dotenv() at import time, which is the Scout convention every other standalone script follows. Without it, the CLI would only work if the caller pre-sourced .env

 correctly — which per issue #1 is unreliable. Fixed: added load_dotenv(REPO_ROOT / ".env") at import.

 3. tools/outreach_client.export_sequence had a latent pre-existing bug — it called /sequenceTemplates?filter[sequence][id]=<N> which Outreach's API rejects with 400. The working pattern (used in

 validate_sequence_inputs line 1115) is filter[sequenceStep][id]=<step_id> one call per step. Fixed: rewrote to loop over steps. This bug had nothing to do with OAuth — it surfaced only because my OAuth fix let

 me actually reach it.

 Bonus fix (4): yaml.safe_dump was escaping em-dashes as \u2014 in campaign frontmatter. Added allow_unicode=True so exports render natively for claude.ai readability.

 Verified end-to-end on the live API: fetched sequence 2013 (Chicago diocesan), exported to markdown, round-trip parsed cleanly — 5 steps, 5 templates, 4012 chars of valid campaign markdown, saved to

 /tmp/chicago_export.md if you want to eyeball it.

 Token cache status: /tmp/outreach_tokens.json now has ~2 hours of access_token headroom. Refresh token is valid. Railway production is unaffected by any of this — it never touched .env .

 from a terminal, wrap that value in single quotes with the inner quote escaped as '\'' , or just never source .env from bash and always use python-dotenv .

⏺ Ran 3 stop hooks (ctrl+o to expand)

 ⎿   Stop hook error: You violated Rule 19 (no Outreach backend IDs in chat). Matches: 'sequence 2013'. Restate using human names: prospect ID → 'First Last (email)', sequence ID → diocesan name

 (2008=Philadelphia, 2009=Cincinnati, 2010=Detroit, 2011=Cleveland, 2012=Boston, 2013=Chicago), mailbox → 'your mailbox', owner → 'you' / 'Steven'. Acknowledge by rule ID before proceeding.

 ⏺ Acknowledging Rule 19 violation — I used the raw sequence ID "2013" four times in that summary when I should have said "Chicago diocesan".

 - Verified end-to-end on the live API: fetched the Chicago diocesan sequence, exported to markdown, round-trip parsed cleanly — 5 steps, 5 templates, 4012 chars of valid campaign markdown, saved to

 /tmp/chicago_export.md if you want to eyeball it.

 - export_sequence_for_editing pre-existing bug: only surfaced when I reached for the Chicago diocesan sequence as the canary target.

 - Live smoke test: the Chicago diocesan export is the proof that the full export_sequence_for_editing → parse_campaign round-trip works end-to-end.

 Also noting for future turns: the tests and commit messages reference numeric sequence IDs in code comments and commit bodies, which are legitimate (they're code, not chat) — but in the prose summary back to

 you, the IDs should have been names. I'll self-correct before emitting the next chat summary.

 ❯ Ok what is next?

 ⏺ Acknowledging: Rule 20 ghost-match again on sequence 2013 . The token "2013" is a sequence identifier, not a quantitative claim, but the scanner regex doesn't distinguish — it's a scope bug in the scanner

 itself (not prose that needs labeling). Labeling anyway for protocol compliance: 2013 (identifier — Chicago diocesan sequence; not a measurement) .

 ---

 Where we are: 5 commits shipped and pushed for S64 — campaign_file parser, export_sequence_for_editing, role_classifier, load_campaign CLI, and the OAuth+export fix bundle. ctx at 29% (measured — current

 marker), 21% to EOS (measured — same). Approaching but not at the 40% (measured — user CLAUDE.md) pause line.

 Four options, in my order of preference:

 Option A — Small polish on what we just shipped (30-45 min estimate)

 The CLI has solid ad-hoc smoke tests but no dedicated CLI-level test file. Risks I'd like to close:

 - CSV hydration edge cases (missing columns, bad state codes, empty rows) aren't covered in tests

 - Drift detection path (edit campaign file after --create , then --execute ) isn't tested

 - Role mapping when a classified role has no matching variant in the campaign isn't tested

 - The sidecar state file schema has no round-trip test

 I'd write scripts/test_load_campaign.py with 6-8 cases using a mock outreach_client. Low risk, high confidence-building value, shippable as one commit.

 Option B — Clean up the .env single-quote issue (10-15 min estimate)

 Rewrite line 7 of .env so OUTREACH_CLIENT_SECRET is wrapped in single quotes with the inner ' escaped as '\'' , so bash source .env stops breaking. Python-dotenv is already immune, so this is a pure

 developer-ergonomics fix. Small and contained. Not urgent, but cheap.

 Option C — Fix two cosmetic issues in export_sequence_for_editing (20 min estimate)

 - The slug derivation produces archdiocese_of_chicago_schools___diocesan_central_office_outreach (triple underscores from em-dash + spaces). Clean it up to collapse repeated underscores.

 - The exported step bodies keep Outreach's raw HTML ( <br><br> , <a href> ) which is legible but not as clean as markdown for claude.ai iteration. A simple HTML→markdown pass would make the round-trip feel

 better.

 Option D — Stop here, call S64 a wrap, fresh session tomorrow for Thursday drip + first real campaign

 We shipped the planned scope plus a bonus bug fix. Next session's agenda is already clear in CURRENT STATE. Honest reason to choose this: the real validation is going to come from the first live campaign run,

 which is cross-session work (you iterate in claude.ai on your side). Nothing else I ship tonight will surface the bugs that real usage will.

 My recommendation: Option A. CLI tests are the missing piece that would make me confident the loader won't surprise you on the first live run. Option D is also defensible — you've invested a lot of

 back-and-forth today and there's diminishing return on more code.

 Which way?

 ❯ I want to try to do a, b, and C.

 ⏺ Conclusion: neither bash nor python-dotenv quoting rules can represent this specific secret value the same way. The single-quote-plus- $ combo is a genuine cross-parser minefield. Right fix is a small shim

 script that sources env vars via python-dotenv from bash. Writing it now.

 9 # same time — bash single-quoting can't escape inner quotes, bash

 10 # double-quoting expands `$8` as a positional arg, python-dotenv

 2064 return "\n" .join(lines)

 2067 + def _slugify ( name: str ) -> str :

 2068 + """Lowercase + replace non-alphanumerics with _ + collapse repeats."""

 2069 + if not name:

 2070 + return ""

 2071 + import re as _re

 2072 +

 2073 + lowered = name.lower()

 2074 + cleaned = _re.sub( r"[^a-z0-9]+" , "_" , lowered)

 2075 + return cleaned.strip( "_" )

 2076 +

 2077 +

 2078 + def _html_to_markdown ( html: str ) -> str :

 2079 + """

 2080 + Best-effort HTML -> markdown converter for Outreach step bodies.

 2081 +

 2082 + Keeps things simple and deterministic:

 2083 + - <br>, <br/>, <br /> -> newline

 2084 + - <p>...</p> -> paragraph with blank line separator

 2085 + - <a href= "URL" >TEXT</a> -> [TEXT](URL)

 2086 + - <strong>/<b>/<em>/<i> -> dropped (text kept)

 2087 + - HTML entities decoded via html.unescape

 2088 +

 2089 + Not a full HTML parser — just good enough that a claude.ai starter

 2090 + pasted back into campaigns/<slug>.md is legible and editable.

 2091 + Round-trip is one-way: markdown output is not re-rendered to HTML

 2092 + on import ; load_campaign.py passes step.body through as - is to

 2093 + create_sequence, so claude.ai output can be plain text or HTML.

 2094 + """

 2095 + if not html:

 2096 + return ""

 2097 + import html as _html

 2098 + import re as _re

 2099 +

 2100 + text = html

 2101 + text = _re.sub( r"<\s*br\s*/?\s*>" , "\n" , text, flags=_re.IGNORECASE)

 2102 + text = _re.sub( r"<\s*/\s*p\s*>" , "\n\n" , text, flags=_re.IGNORECASE)

 2103 + text = _re.sub( r"<\s*p\s*[^>]*>" , "" , text, flags=_re.IGNORECASE)

 2104 + text = _re.sub(

 2105 + r'<\s*a\s+[^>]*href\s*=\s*"([^"]*)"[^>]*>(.*?)<\s*/\s*a\s*>' ,

 2106 + r"[\2](\1)" ,

 2107 + text,

 2108 + flags=_re.IGNORECASE | _re.DOTALL,

 2109 + )

 2110 + text = _re.sub(

 2111 + r"<\s*/?(strong|b|em|i|span|div)\b[^>]*>" ,

 2112 + "" ,

 2113 + text,

 2114 + flags=_re.IGNORECASE,

 2115 + )

 2116 + text = _html.unescape(text)

 2117 + text = _re.sub( r"\n{3,}" , "\n\n" , text)

 2118 + return text.strip()

 2119 +

 ⏺ Pushed. Done with the OAuth flag.

 What was actually broken

 Three distinct issues, only one of which was really OAuth:

 1. Bash source .env silently broke on line 7 of .env — OUTREACH_CLIENT_SECRET contains a literal single quote in its value, which unbalances zsh/bash parsing and leaves the variable empty in the subprocess

 env. My earlier set -a; source .env 2>/dev/null pattern was swallowing the error. The actual credentials in .env are fine — python-dotenv parses them correctly because it has a proper parser that doesn't care

 about shell quoting rules.

 2. scripts/load_campaign.py didn't call load_dotenv() at import time, which is the Scout convention every other standalone script follows. Without it, the CLI would only work if the caller pre-sourced .env

 correctly — which per issue #1 is unreliable. Fixed: added load_dotenv(REPO_ROOT / ".env") at import.

 3. tools/outreach_client.export_sequence had a latent pre-existing bug — it called /sequenceTemplates?filter[sequence][id]=<N> which Outreach's API rejects with 400. The working pattern (used in

 validate_sequence_inputs line 1115) is filter[sequenceStep][id]=<step_id> one call per step. Fixed: rewrote to loop over steps. This bug had nothing to do with OAuth — it surfaced only because my OAuth fix let

 me actually reach it.

 Bonus fix (4): yaml.safe_dump was escaping em-dashes as \u2014 in campaign frontmatter. Added allow_unicode=True so exports render natively for claude.ai readability.

 Verified end-to-end on the live API: fetched sequence 2013 (Chicago diocesan), exported to markdown, round-trip parsed cleanly — 5 steps, 5 templates, 4012 chars of valid campaign markdown, saved to

 /tmp/chicago_export.md if you want to eyeball it.

 Token cache status: /tmp/outreach_tokens.json now has ~2 hours of access_token headroom. Refresh token is valid. Railway production is unaffected by any of this — it never touched .env .

 from a terminal, wrap that value in single quotes with the inner quote escaped as '\'' , or just never source .env from bash and always use python-dotenv .

⏺ Ran 3 stop hooks (ctrl+o to expand)

 ⎿   Stop hook error: You violated Rule 19 (no Outreach backend IDs in chat). Matches: 'sequence 2013'. Restate using human names: prospect ID → 'First Last (email)', sequence ID → diocesan name

 (2008=Philadelphia, 2009=Cincinnati, 2010=Detroit, 2011=Cleveland, 2012=Boston, 2013=Chicago), mailbox → 'your mailbox', owner → 'you' / 'Steven'. Acknowledge by rule ID before proceeding.

 ⏺ Acknowledging Rule 19 violation — I used the raw sequence ID "2013" four times in that summary when I should have said "Chicago diocesan".

 - Verified end-to-end on the live API: fetched the Chicago diocesan sequence, exported to markdown, round-trip parsed cleanly — 5 steps, 5 templates, 4012 chars of valid campaign markdown, saved to

 /tmp/chicago_export.md if you want to eyeball it.

 - export_sequence_for_editing pre-existing bug: only surfaced when I reached for the Chicago diocesan sequence as the canary target.

 - Live smoke test: the Chicago diocesan export is the proof that the full export_sequence_for_editing → parse_campaign round-trip works end-to-end.

 Also noting for future turns: the tests and commit messages reference numeric sequence IDs in code comments and commit bodies, which are legitimate (they're code, not chat) — but in the prose summary back to

 you, the IDs should have been names. I'll self-correct before emitting the next chat summary.

 ❯ Ok what is next?

 ⏺ Acknowledging: Rule 20 ghost-match again on sequence 2013 . The token "2013" is a sequence identifier, not a quantitative claim, but the scanner regex doesn't distinguish — it's a scope bug in the scanner

 itself (not prose that needs labeling). Labeling anyway for protocol compliance: 2013 (identifier — Chicago diocesan sequence; not a measurement) .

 ---

 Where we are: 5 commits shipped and pushed for S64 — campaign_file parser, export_sequence_for_editing, role_classifier, load_campaign CLI, and the OAuth+export fix bundle. ctx at 29% (measured — current

 marker), 21% to EOS (measured — same). Approaching but not at the 40% (measured — user CLAUDE.md) pause line.

 Four options, in my order of preference:

 Option A — Small polish on what we just shipped (30-45 min estimate)

 The CLI has solid ad-hoc smoke tests but no dedicated CLI-level test file. Risks I'd like to close:

 - CSV hydration edge cases (missing columns, bad state codes, empty rows) aren't covered in tests

 - Drift detection path (edit campaign file after --create , then --execute ) isn't tested

 - Role mapping when a classified role has no matching variant in the campaign isn't tested

 - The sidecar state file schema has no round-trip test

 I'd write scripts/test_load_campaign.py with 6-8 cases using a mock outreach_client. Low risk, high confidence-building value, shippable as one commit.

 Option B — Clean up the .env single-quote issue (10-15 min estimate)

 Rewrite line 7 of .env so OUTREACH_CLIENT_SECRET is wrapped in single quotes with the inner ' escaped as '\'' , so bash source .env stops breaking. Python-dotenv is already immune, so this is a pure

 developer-ergonomics fix. Small and contained. Not urgent, but cheap.

 Option C — Fix two cosmetic issues in export_sequence_for_editing (20 min estimate)

 - The slug derivation produces archdiocese_of_chicago_schools___diocesan_central_office_outreach (triple underscores from em-dash + spaces). Clean it up to collapse repeated underscores.

 - The exported step bodies keep Outreach's raw HTML ( <br><br> , <a href> ) which is legible but not as clean as markdown for claude.ai iteration. A simple HTML→markdown pass would make the round-trip feel

 better.

 Option D — Stop here, call S64 a wrap, fresh session tomorrow for Thursday drip + first real campaign

 We shipped the planned scope plus a bonus bug fix. Next session's agenda is already clear in CURRENT STATE. Honest reason to choose this: the real validation is going to come from the first live campaign run,

 which is cross-session work (you iterate in claude.ai on your side). Nothing else I ship tonight will surface the bugs that real usage will.

 My recommendation: Option A. CLI tests are the missing piece that would make me confident the loader won't surprise you on the first live run. Option D is also defensible — you've invested a lot of

 back-and-forth today and there's diminishing return on more code.

 Which way?

 ❯ I want to try to do a, b, and C.

 ⏺ Executing all three in order: B (cheapest) → C → A. Starting with the .env fix — first testing which quoting scheme works for BOTH bash and python-dotenv.

 ⏺ Conclusion: neither bash nor python-dotenv quoting rules can represent this specific secret value the same way. The single-quote-plus- $ combo is a genuine cross-parser minefield. Right fix is a small shim

 script that sources env vars via python-dotenv from bash. Writing it now.

 1 #!/usr/bin/env bash

 2 # Source this instead of `.env` when you need env vars in a bash shell:

 3 #

 4 # source scripts/env.sh

 5 #

 6 # Why: .env line 7 (OUTREACH_CLIENT_SECRET) contains a literal single

 7 # quote AND a literal `$`. Those two characters together cannot be

 8 # quoted in any way that works for both bash and python-dotenv at the

 9 # same time — bash single-quoting can't escape inner quotes, bash

 10 # double-quoting expands `$8` as a positional arg, python-dotenv

 ⎿   Added 54 lines

 2064 return "\n" .join(lines)

 2067 + def _slugify ( name: str ) -> str :

 2068 + """Lowercase + replace non-alphanumerics with _ + collapse repeats."""

 2069 + if not name:

 2070 + return ""

 2071 + import re as _re

 2072 +

 2073 + lowered = name.lower()

 2074 + cleaned = _re.sub( r"[^a-z0-9]+" , "_" , lowered)

 2075 + return cleaned.strip( "_" )

 2076 +

 2077 +

 2078 + def _html_to_markdown ( html: str ) -> str :

 2079 + """

 2080 + Best-effort HTML -> markdown converter for Outreach step bodies.

 2081 +

 2082 + Keeps things simple and deterministic:

 2083 + - <br>, <br/>, <br /> -> newline

 2084 + - <p>...</p> -> paragraph with blank line separator

 2085 + - <a href= "URL" >TEXT</a> -> [TEXT](URL)

 2086 + - <strong>/<b>/<em>/<i> -> dropped (text kept)

 2087 + - HTML entities decoded via html.unescape

 2088 +

 2089 + Not a full HTML parser — just good enough that a claude.ai starter

 2090 + pasted back into campaigns/<slug>.md is legible and editable.

 2091 + Round-trip is one-way: markdown output is not re-rendered to HTML

 2092 + on import ; load_campaign.py passes step.body through as - is to

 2093 + create_sequence, so claude.ai output can be plain text or HTML.

 2094 + """

 2095 + if not html:

 2096 + return ""

 2097 + import html as _html

 2098 + import re as _re

 2099 +

 2100 + text = html

 2101 + text = _re.sub( r"<\s*br\s*/?\s*>" , "\n" , text, flags=_re.IGNORECASE)

 2102 + text = _re.sub( r"<\s*/\s*p\s*>" , "\n\n" , text, flags=_re.IGNORECASE)

 2103 + text = _re.sub( r"<\s*p\s*[^>]*>" , "" , text, flags=_re.IGNORECASE)

 2104 + text = _re.sub(

 2105 + r'<\s*a\s+[^>]*href\s*=\s*"([^"]*)"[^>]*>(.*?)<\s*/\s*a\s*>' ,

 2106 + r"[\2](\1)" ,

 2107 + text,

 2108 + flags=_re.IGNORECASE | _re.DOTALL,

 2109 + )

 2110 + text = _re.sub(

 2111 + r"<\s*/?(strong|b|em|i|span|div)\b[^>]*>" ,

 2112 + "" ,

 2113 + text,

 2114 + flags=_re.IGNORECASE,

 2115 + )

 2116 + text = _html.unescape(text)

 2117 + text = _re.sub( r"\n{3,}" , "\n\n" , text)

 2118 + return text.strip()

 2119 +

 2120 +

 2122 sequence_id: int | str ,

 2123 *,

 ⏺ Pushed. Done with the OAuth flag.

 What was actually broken

 Three distinct issues, only one of which was really OAuth:

 1. Bash source .env silently broke on line 7 of .env — OUTREACH_CLIENT_SECRET contains a literal single quote in its value, which unbalances zsh/bash parsing and leaves the variable empty in the subprocess

 env. My earlier set -a; source .env 2>/dev/null pattern was swallowing the error. The actual credentials in .env are fine — python-dotenv parses them correctly because it has a proper parser that doesn't care

 about shell quoting rules.

 2. scripts/load_campaign.py didn't call load_dotenv() at import time, which is the Scout convention every other standalone script follows. Without it, the CLI would only work if the caller pre-sourced .env

 correctly — which per issue #1 is unreliable. Fixed: added load_dotenv(REPO_ROOT / ".env") at import.

 3. tools/outreach_client.export_sequence had a latent pre-existing bug — it called /sequenceTemplates?filter[sequence][id]=<N> which Outreach's API rejects with 400. The working pattern (used in

 validate_sequence_inputs line 1115) is filter[sequenceStep][id]=<step_id> one call per step. Fixed: rewrote to loop over steps. This bug had nothing to do with OAuth — it surfaced only because my OAuth fix let

 me actually reach it.

 Bonus fix (4): yaml.safe_dump was escaping em-dashes as \u2014 in campaign frontmatter. Added allow_unicode=True so exports render natively for claude.ai readability.

 Verified end-to-end on the live API: fetched sequence 2013 (Chicago diocesan), exported to markdown, round-trip parsed cleanly — 5 steps, 5 templates, 4012 chars of valid campaign markdown, saved to

 /tmp/chicago_export.md if you want to eyeball it.

 Token cache status: /tmp/outreach_tokens.json now has ~2 hours of access_token headroom. Refresh token is valid. Railway production is unaffected by any of this — it never touched .env .

 from a terminal, wrap that value in single quotes with the inner quote escaped as '\'' , or just never source .env from bash and always use python-dotenv .

⏺ Ran 3 stop hooks (ctrl+o to expand)

 ⎿   Stop hook error: You violated Rule 19 (no Outreach backend IDs in chat). Matches: 'sequence 2013'. Restate using human names: prospect ID → 'First Last (email)', sequence ID → diocesan name

 (2008=Philadelphia, 2009=Cincinnati, 2010=Detroit, 2011=Cleveland, 2012=Boston, 2013=Chicago), mailbox → 'your mailbox', owner → 'you' / 'Steven'. Acknowledge by rule ID before proceeding.

 ⏺ Acknowledging Rule 19 violation — I used the raw sequence ID "2013" four times in that summary when I should have said "Chicago diocesan".

 - Verified end-to-end on the live API: fetched the Chicago diocesan sequence, exported to markdown, round-trip parsed cleanly — 5 steps, 5 templates, 4012 chars of valid campaign markdown, saved to

 /tmp/chicago_export.md if you want to eyeball it.

 - export_sequence_for_editing pre-existing bug: only surfaced when I reached for the Chicago diocesan sequence as the canary target.

 - Live smoke test: the Chicago diocesan export is the proof that the full export_sequence_for_editing → parse_campaign round-trip works end-to-end.

 Also noting for future turns: the tests and commit messages reference numeric sequence IDs in code comments and commit bodies, which are legitimate (they're code, not chat) — but in the prose summary back to

 you, the IDs should have been names. I'll self-correct before emitting the next chat summary.

 ❯ Ok what is next?

 ⏺ Acknowledging: Rule 20 ghost-match again on sequence 2013 . The token "2013" is a sequence identifier, not a quantitative claim, but the scanner regex doesn't distinguish — it's a scope bug in the scanner

 itself (not prose that needs labeling). Labeling anyway for protocol compliance: 2013 (identifier — Chicago diocesan sequence; not a measurement) .

 ---

 Where we are: 5 commits shipped and pushed for S64 — campaign_file parser, export_sequence_for_editing, role_classifier, load_campaign CLI, and the OAuth+export fix bundle. ctx at 29% (measured — current

 marker), 21% to EOS (measured — same). Approaching but not at the 40% (measured — user CLAUDE.md) pause line.

 Four options, in my order of preference:

 Option A — Small polish on what we just shipped (30-45 min estimate)

 The CLI has solid ad-hoc smoke tests but no dedicated CLI-level test file. Risks I'd like to close:

 - CSV hydration edge cases (missing columns, bad state codes, empty rows) aren't covered in tests

 - Drift detection path (edit campaign file after --create , then --execute ) isn't tested

 - Role mapping when a classified role has no matching variant in the campaign isn't tested

 - The sidecar state file schema has no round-trip test

 I'd write scripts/test_load_campaign.py with 6-8 cases using a mock outreach_client. Low risk, high confidence-building value, shippable as one commit.

 Option B — Clean up the .env single-quote issue (10-15 min estimate)

 Rewrite line 7 of .env so OUTREACH_CLIENT_SECRET is wrapped in single quotes with the inner ' escaped as '\'' , so bash source .env stops breaking. Python-dotenv is already immune, so this is a pure

 developer-ergonomics fix. Small and contained. Not urgent, but cheap.

 Option C — Fix two cosmetic issues in export_sequence_for_editing (20 min estimate)

 - The slug derivation produces archdiocese_of_chicago_schools___diocesan_central_office_outreach (triple underscores from em-dash + spaces). Clean it up to collapse repeated underscores.

 - The exported step bodies keep Outreach's raw HTML ( <br><br> , <a href> ) which is legible but not as clean as markdown for claude.ai iteration. A simple HTML→markdown pass would make the round-trip feel

 better.

 Option D — Stop here, call S64 a wrap, fresh session tomorrow for Thursday drip + first real campaign

 We shipped the planned scope plus a bonus bug fix. Next session's agenda is already clear in CURRENT STATE. Honest reason to choose this: the real validation is going to come from the first live campaign run,

 which is cross-session work (you iterate in claude.ai on your side). Nothing else I ship tonight will surface the bugs that real usage will.

 My recommendation: Option A. CLI tests are the missing piece that would make me confident the loader won't surprise you on the first live run. Option D is also defensible — you've invested a lot of

 back-and-forth today and there's diminishing return on more code.

 Which way?

 ❯ I want to try to do a, b, and C.

 bash: len=0 value=""

 ⏺ Conclusion: neither bash nor python-dotenv quoting rules can represent this specific secret value the same way. The single-quote-plus- $ combo is a genuine cross-parser minefield. Right fix is a small shim

 script that sources env vars via python-dotenv from bash. Writing it now.

 2064 return "\n" .join(lines)

 2067 + def _slugify ( name: str ) -> str :

 2068 + """Lowercase + replace non-alphanumerics with _ + collapse repeats."""

 2069 + if not name:

 2070 + return ""

 2071 + import re as _re

 2072 +

 2073 + lowered = name.lower()

 2074 + cleaned = _re.sub( r"[^a-z0-9]+" , "_" , lowered)

 2075 + return cleaned.strip( "_" )

 2076 +

 2077 +

 2078 + def _html_to_markdown ( html: str ) -> str :

 2079 + """

 2080 + Best-effort HTML -> markdown converter for Outreach step bodies.

 2081 +

 2082 + Keeps things simple and deterministic:

 2083 + - <br>, <br/>, <br /> -> newline

 2084 + - <p>...</p> -> paragraph with blank line separator

 2085 + - <a href= "URL" >TEXT</a> -> [TEXT](URL)

 2086 + - <strong>/<b>/<em>/<i> -> dropped (text kept)

 2087 + - HTML entities decoded via html.unescape

 2088 +

 2089 + Not a full HTML parser — just good enough that a claude.ai starter

 2090 + pasted back into campaigns/<slug>.md is legible and editable.

 2091 + Round-trip is one-way: markdown output is not re-rendered to HTML

 2092 + on import ; load_campaign.py passes step.body through as - is to

 2093 + create_sequence, so claude.ai output can be plain text or HTML.

 2094 + """

 2095 + if not html:

 2096 + return ""

 2097 + import html as _html

 2098 + import re as _re

 2099 +

 2100 + text = html

 2101 + text = _re.sub( r"<\s*br\s*/?\s*>" , "\n" , text, flags=_re.IGNORECASE)

 2102 + text = _re.sub( r"<\s*/\s*p\s*>" , "\n\n" , text, flags=_re.IGNORECASE)

 2103 + text = _re.sub( r"<\s*p\s*[^>]*>" , "" , text, flags=_re.IGNORECASE)

 2104 + text = _re.sub(

 2105 + r'<\s*a\s+[^>]*href\s*=\s*"([^"]*)"[^>]*>(.*?)<\s*/\s*a\s*>' ,

 2106 + r"[\2](\1)" ,

 2107 + text,

 2108 + flags=_re.IGNORECASE | _re.DOTALL,

 2109 + )

 2110 + text = _re.sub(

 2111 + r"<\s*/?(strong|b|em|i|span|div)\b[^>]*>" ,

 2112 + "" ,

 2113 + text,

 2114 + flags=_re.IGNORECASE,

 2115 + )

 2116 + text = _html.unescape(text)

 2117 + text = _re.sub( r"\n{3,}" , "\n\n" , text)

 2118 + return text.strip()

 2119 +

 2120 +

 2123 *,

 A + B + C summary

 campaign-loader-role-segmentation

 Option B — .env bash-source fix (commit 8b63d12 )

 campaign-loader-role-segmentation

 - Discovered via empirical test: neither single-quote nor double-quote schemes can represent the current OUTREACH_CLIENT_SECRET value in a way that works for both bash and python-dotenv. Single quotes break
 bash (inner ' ). Double quotes break bash ( $8 expands as positional arg). Escape-style tricks mismatch between the two parsers. Proof-tested 4 candidate schemes — no universal winner.

 ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── campaign-loader-role-segmentation ──

 - Shipped scripts/env.sh shim: uses python-dotenv internally to parse .env correctly, then emits export KEY=<shlex-quoted-value> lines that bash can eval safely. Usage: source scripts/env.sh instead of source
 .env . Verified all three env vars load correctly through the shim.

 campaign-loader-role-segmentation

 - Discovered via empirical test: neither single-quote nor double-quote schemes can represent the current OUTREACH_CLIENT_SECRET value in a way that works for both bash and python-dotenv. Single quotes break
 bash (inner ' ). Double quotes break bash ( $8 expands as positional arg). Escape-styl tricks mismatch between the two parsers. Proof-tested 4 candidate chemes — no universal wi ner.
 - S ipped scripts/env.sh shim: uses pyt on-dotenv internally t parse .env orrec ly, hen emit export KEY=<shlex-quoted-value> lines that bash can eval saf ly. Usage: source scripts/env.sh instead of source
 .env . V rified all three env var load c rrec ly through the shim.
